title: Hobbies, Careers, and STEM: How Passions Shape What We Do
description: Choosing a major or career isn’t just about job prospects—it’s about what excites you! In this talk, we’ll explore how hobbies can influence STEM pathways, and spark ideas. From tinkering and crafting to coding and experimenting, I’ll share how personal interests have fueled my projects and papers, and how you can turn what you love into what you do. Expect stories, laughs, and a fresh perspective on why your passions matter in STEM. Please register to attend this hybrid event.

title: Great Decisions
description: Join us at Great Decisions for a lively discussion of major events playing out now across the world stage. Expand your understanding of history in the making.Each year eight issues are chosen and briefed by the non-partisan Foreign Policy Association for this program. Topics this year include Middle East realignment, climate technology, the U.S. – China trade rivalry and more. Participants read an article in the Foreign Policy Association briefing book, which can be purchased for $30 or borrowed from the library.  We meet, view a related video, and discuss the briefed questions on eight consecutive Wednesday afternoons.Please send questions to Peggy Slater, peggyslater@msn.com with a subject line of Great Decisions. For more information on the Great Decisions program see https://www.fpa.org/great_decisions. Please register for this event by contacting Peggy Slater at peggyslater@msn.com with a subject line of Great Decisions.  The Great Decisions series is sponsored by the League of Women Voters of Winnetka-Northfield-Kenilworth in cooperation with the Winnetka-Northfield Public Library District.

title: Religious Studies Speaker Series: Vaughn A. Booker
description:
Black Sacred Jazz Histories

What is the social, cultural, and political authority of Black churches for the people whom these institutions shaped, but who chose to pursue their own lives outside of these institutional walls, without making the leap to other religious traditions? What is authority for those who don’t formally identify with Black churches? Jazz is an artistic profession where Black musicians in the era of Jim Crow and within the dominant culture of what was then referred to as the “Negro church”—now "the Black church"—viewed themselves as representatives of their race. Several musicians also crafted expressions of religion through their artistry. This talk will showcase how some prominent jazz artists, including Duke Ellington and Mary Lou Williams, agreed with, or diverged from, the social and theological norms of Black Protestant church culture through their sacred music, which also reflected their conceptions of African American sacred history.

Please register to attend this virtual event.

title: Music Theater Works: Cats
description:
Music and Lyrics by Andrew Lloyd Webber
Based on “Old Possum’s Book of Practical Cats” by T.S. Eliot
Directed and Choreographed by Mandy Modic
Music Directed by Linda Madonia
Music Theater Works kicks off its 46th season with CATS, Andrew Lloyd Webber’s electrifying, Tony Award-winning musical. In this immersive production set in a larger-than-life British flat, audiences may join the magical and all encompassing world of the Jellicle cats as they gather for the annual Jellicle Ball, where one will be chosen for a new life. Through a series of dazzling musical numbers, each cat shares their story— from the mischievous Rum Tum Tugger, to the mysterious Macavity, and the once-glamorous Grizabella. Featuring unforgettable songs, breathtaking choreography, and a whimsical atmosphere, CATS is a celebration of life, community and the power of memory

description:

description:

We are hosting Becca Lubow, an organizer with JCUA and leader of JCUA’s  Immigration Justice Committee, who will provide an update on the current legislative landscape in Illinois including the launch of the 2026 ICIRR Immigration Justice State Platform. Rabbi London will also be speaking, opening the session with a teaching. Leave with more knowledge and concrete ways to take action in support of immigrant communities in our state! You may join us in person at the synagogue, or via Zoom.

title: Financial Literacy and Wellbeing Workshops - TRIO Scholarship Workshop with Office of Student Financial Assistance and Educational Foundation
description:
The financial literacy workshops aim to inform TRIO students of the financial aid process within a college/university setting. Students will have an opportunity to learn about the various types of financial aid available. These workshops will prepare students to prepare, complete, and/or renew their FAFSA, learn how to budget their finances and build their personal credit.

TRIO will aim to educate students on making financial decisions that will help them throughout their college careers and in their personal lives. In conjunction with the workshops, TRIO will introduce GradReady to TRIO students and provide topics for review on personal finances, loans, and budgeting.

title: Ciné Club
description:
Alliance Française du North Shore
Join a group of *cinéphiles* online for a discussion in French of a French film with English subtitles.
The choice for this month is *Diva*. You'll watch the film on your own: view it for free on [Kanopy](https://www.kanopy.com/en/). For more details, write to cine-club@AFnorthshore.org.
**Diva (1981)**
**Un film de Jean-Jacques Beine**ix
Jules. Frédéric Andrei
Cynthia Hawkins. Wilhelmina Fernandez
Alba. They An Lu
Jean Saporta. Jacques Fabbri
Le curé Dominique Piñon
L’antillais Gérard Darmon
Questions to kick off our discussion:
1.) La musique que chante Cynthia Hawkins est une aria de ‘La Wally’, un opéra d'Alfredo Catalani (1892). L’opéra est rarement joué à l’exception de cet aria. Que pensez-vous de cette musique? Dans l’article ci-joint, l’auteur affirme qu'on ressent la musique différemment chaque fois qu’on l'entend. Êtes-vous d’accord?

2\. A mon avis\, le style du film est plus ‘punk’ que ‘classique’ \(les personnages\, les vêtements\, la mise en scène\)\. Pourquoi ont\-ils choisi que la musique préférée de Jules est l’opéra?

3\. Le film montre de nombreux sites célèbres à Paris\. Quels sites avez\-vous reconnus?

4\. Plusieurs des emplacements utilisés étaient en ruines \(comme l’appartement de Jules\, le Théâtre du Châtelet\)\. Pourquoi?

5\. La poursuite dans le métro est très célèbre\. Qu’en pensez\-vous?

6\. Pourquoi Cynthia ne voulait\-t\-elle pas enregistrer sa musique?

7\. Que pensez\-vous du personnage d'Alba?

8\. Diva est classifiée comme faisant partie du "cinéma du look’" un mouvement des années 1980 avec ces caractéristiques:\-

A. La prédominance du spectacle visuel comparée à la substance narrative
B. Les personnages sont de jeunes marginaux
C. Le métro est souvent présent comme symbole d’une société alternative
D. Les cultures pop d’une part et plus élitistes d’autre part sont mélangées

Êtes vous d'accord avec cette classification pour Diva? Avez-vous trouvé une scène particulèrement mémorable? Laquelle? Pourquoi?

9\. Recommanderiez\-vous ce film à vos amis?

htt*ps://operawire.com/opera-meets-film-how-la-wallys-most-famous-aria-expresses-vast-array-of-emotions-throughout-diva/*

**Membership in the AFduNS**
While no membership is required for newcomers, we welcome new members whose membership will support the activities of the AFduNS, a nonprofit 501(c)(3) organization. For further info, visit our [website](https://afnorthshore.org/) or [contact us](https://afnorthshore.org/contact-us). If you wish to join the AFduNS, you may sign up [online](https://afnorthshore.org/join). Annual dues are $50.00

title: Thursday Trivia Night at Stacked and Folded in Evanston!
description:
Evanston Trivia Nerds Meetup!
WE WON 1ST LAST 2 TIMES!!!! This is our new fav place for trivia. Had plenty of space, great food, chill lighting, good trivia host and all questions show on your phone and leaderboard and on the tv.

Trivia starts at 7PM and I think some of us should try to get there at 6:45. There are 2 sessions and each are 1 hour long. As usual, trivia is free so please either order dinner or at least grab a drink or two to support the business! Both the food and drinks are delish!

This event is hosted by a trivia company called Sporcle. Info on how Sporcle live trivia works: https://www.sporcle.com/blog/2015/02/bar-trivia-what-is-sporcle-live/

It's right off the Noyes Purple Line El stop and there should be some street parking.

Looking forward to meeting folks and assembling the dream team! :D

PLEASE NOTE: IF YOU RSVP AND CAN'T ATTEND, PLEASE BE COURTEOUS AND CHANGE TO "NOT GOING" SO A SLOT IS AVAILABLE FOR OTHERS. IF YOU DON'T UN-RSVP OR MESSAGE ME, I WILL HAVE TO REMOVE YOU FROM THE GROUP.

\~Suzie

description: You’re invited to an evening of conversation and mingling in celebration of the Lincolnwood Reads 2026 selection, How to Read a Book by Monica Wood. This is a book for book lovers, so whether you’ve finished it, still have it on your to-read list, or have been busy reading other things--join us!We’ll have live music played by local celebrated harpist Nichole Young. Guests can also enjoy small bites, mocktails, and alcoholic beverages including beer and wine. Registration is required and limited to Lincolnwood residents, who may add up to two guests (non-resident guests welcome) to their registration. All attendees must be 21 or older and provide a valid ID before entering the event. Doors open at 6pm.

title: Family Movie
description: Freakier FridayWhat better way to celebrate Friday the 13th than watching the follow-up to Freaky Friday, the 2003 Lindsay Lohan and Jamie Lee Curtis classic? Indeed, more than 20 years have passed since mother and daughter switched bodies and literally lived the other’s life. It turns out to be a family thing. This sequel has even more body swapping than the original and you’ll love that they include a nod to Lindsay Lohan's The Parent Trap. Starring Jamie Lee Curtis, Lindsay Lohan, Julia Butters, and Sophia Hammons. 2025 : 111 minutes : PG for thematic elements, rude humor, language and some suggestive references : ComedyWatch the trailer for Freakier Friday.Find Freakier Friday in the library catalog.

location: 6140 Lincoln Avenue, Morton Grove, IL, 60053, US
description: Watch this critically acclaimed film, enjoy complimentary popcorn, and have a chance to win door prizes. title: Music Theater Works: Cats
description:
Music and Lyrics by Andrew Lloyd Webber
Based on “Old Possum’s Book of Practical Cats” by T.S. Eliot
Directed and Choreographed by Mandy Modic
Music Directed by Linda Madonia
Music Theater Works kicks off its 46th season with CATS, Andrew Lloyd Webber’s electrifying, Tony Award-winning musical. In this immersive production set in a larger-than-life British flat, audiences may join the magical and all encompassing world of the Jellicle cats as they gather for the annual Jellicle Ball, where one will be chosen for a new life. Through a series of dazzling musical numbers, each cat shares their story— from the mischievous Rum Tum Tugger, to the mysterious Macavity, and the once-glamorous Grizabella. Featuring unforgettable songs, breathtaking choreography, and a whimsical atmosphere, CATS is a celebration of life, community and the power of memory

title: NASINNIA
description: The Hromovytsia Ukrainian Dance Ensemble welcomes the spring season with our newest production, NASINNIA. Inspired by the imagery of wheat – a foundational symbol of life, endurance, and beauty of the Ukrainian spirit – this production honors the rich history of Ukraine, the resilience of our ancestors, and the eternal cycle of growth and strength held within every seed we plant across generations. Join us for this new chapter as we celebrate storytelling through dance and honor the birth of new beginnings.

title: Sunday Morning Minyan
description:
The Sunday Morning Minyan meets in the Beit Midrash at Beth Emet.
Join us on Sundays to daven (pray) the weekday Shacharit (morning) service!  First-timers are encouraged! Please note, we will continue to Zoom as well for virtual participation.
The Minyan offers an intimate opportunity to pray with the Beth Emet community. The service explores our ritual, liturgical, and musical traditions in a welcoming setting for people new to prayer while providing a deeper connection for people with more experience.
Please contact the office for a link.
Participants are encouraged to wear a tallit and tefillin.

description:
Conversational Hebrew meets most Sundays, 11:00 am–12:00 pm when Beit Sefer meets. This class uses an Ulpan-like immersive setting to teach modern conversational Hebrew. Participants will read news articles, discuss life and current events, and other basic conversations in Hebrew. The only prerequisite is basic Hebrew skills. The class meets in the Beit Midrash.
Members, click here to register.
Guests, click here to register.
Dorit Flatt is the daughter of Holocaust survivors and grew up in Tel-Aviv, Israel. After completing her IDF service in the paratrooper unit, she studied elementary education at Seminar Levinsky in Tel-Aviv. She holds a BA in Psychology from the University of Michigan and taught Hebrew language, literature, and religious studies in Michigan for 25 years.

description: Join multi-Emmy Award winning producer and editor Harvey Moshman for a screening of a 1-hour special of the PBS series Wild Travels. Whether you’re inspired to walk by the STAND STILL PARADE in Whalan, Minnesota or enter your facial hair in the INTERNATIONAL BEARD & MUSTACHE COMPETITION in Portland, Oregon (ladies welcome!), AMERICA'S MOST UNUSUAL FESTIVALS is your VIP pass to the craziest annual celebrations that the US of A has to offer. Screening followed by live Q&A.This presentation is CANCELLED for March 15, 2026. It is rescheduled for March 29th, 2026.

title: Introduction to Hebrew
description:
Introduction to Hebrew meets most Sundays, 12:15–1:15 pm when Beit Sefer meets. This beginner class is an opportunity for learners with no or limited Hebrew background to become familiar with the Hebrew alphabet, how to sound out Hebrew words, and begin to develop reading proficiency. You will also acquire some basic Hebrew vocabulary that connects us to Jewish life, ritual, tradition, and Israel. This class meets a requirement for Adult Kabbalat Mitzvah and is set up in Room 20.
Members, click here to register.
Guests, click here to register.
Bluma Stoller is a Beth Emet member and a graduate of Columbia University and the Jewish Theological Seminary. She was the North American Director of Academic Affairs for Tel Aviv University and was the recipient of Hillel International’s Exemplar of Excellence Award for her commitment to social justice and tikkun olam. She has held leadership positions in major Jewish organizations, including Project Otzma and the JCRC of Greater Boston.

title: 3/15 @ 1PM - Sunday Games at Sketchbook Evanston
description:
Evanston Board Games Meetup
Join us at Sketchbook Brewing Company in Evanston for our Sunday Board Game Meetup!

We meet most Sundays at Sketchbook. Just look for the group in the back that has stacks of board games to pick from! We welcome people of all skill levels to come and play, as we always teach new games to people that have not played them before.

Feel free to bring your own games that you want to teach and are familiar with. A typical event starts with talking about what we brought that week, and then we break out into smaller groups to play games based on player count and what everyone is interested in. It's common to have 2 or 3 games going at once, and fitting in a second or third game before the event ends at 4pm. Please RSVP and be on time so that we can include you at the start of the first game, and buy a drink to support the venue!

title: NSC Presents: Peppa Pig - My First Concert
description:
PEPPA PIG: MY FIRST CONCERT
From Mozart to Muddy Puddles
Peppa Pig, along with Mummy Pig, Daddy Pig, George and new baby sister Evie, are coming to SKOKIE for PEPPA PIG: MY FIRST CONCERT – a fun, interactive introduction to a live orchestra for ages 18 months and older. Join Peppa and George to learn all about the different sounds that instruments make together! Enjoy some of your favorite music from the show played by a live orchestra and discover some other exciting orchestral pieces perfect for children. Plus, some little piggies can join Peppa and her family to make music of their own!
Add a Meet and Greet Experience
Enhance your visit with a post-performance meet and greet with a character from Peppa Pig – My First Concert. The package includes a photo opportunity with the character, a plush toy, and other surprises.

title: The Little Dancer: A Reimagining of Le Petit Prince (R)
location: 4000 W. Pratt Ave., Lincolnwood, IL, 60712, US
description: Ede2 celebrates its 15th Anniversary with an original dance production “The Little Dancer: A Reimagining of Le Petit Prince” written by Antoine de Saint-Exupéry. The out-of-this-world production will follow the story of a pilot marooned in the desert when they meet the mysterious stranger who traveled to Earth from a far away asteroid in search of the meaning of connection and friendship. This family-friendly show features an eccentric cast of characters who populate various planets, such as space royalty, a talking rose and a friendly fox, who all teach The Little Dancer unique lessons on becoming a good person and how to care for others.All ages are welcome to attend. Children must be accompanied by a caregiver.

title: The Dark Side of the Eiffel Tower
description:
Alliance Française du North Shore
Holly Clayson will explore little-known, forgotten and erased aspects of the Eiffel Tower's identity when it first arose in the French capital in 1889. The tallest structure on Earth, it was the centerpiece of that year's Exposition Universelle. She will discuss the Tower's use of profuse iron ornament and illumination as surveillance alongside a consideration of its function as the emblem par excellence of Republican Imperialism. She will also discuss the imagery of the Tower in the visual arts.

S. Hollis “Holly” Clayson was Professor of Art History at Northwestern University for 35 years and retired in 2020 as Professor Emerita of Art History and Bergen Evans Professor Emerita in the Humanities.

**Program Registration for Members of the AF DuPage: Free**
If you're a member of the AF DuPage and are attending a monthly program for the first time, please RSVP to programmes@AFnorthshore.org with “DuPage” in the subject line. Include your name and Zoom screen name, if different. If you're a member of our Meetup group, we'll mark you as such and you'll no longer need to send RSVP emails as long as you RSVP on Meetup.
If you're not a member of the AFduNS Meetup group, you'll need to send an email RSVP for each monthly program.

**Program Registration for Nonmembers: $10**
If you're not a member of the AFduNS or the AF DuPage, the cost of attending this program is $10. [Click here](https://forms.gle/f89WGYhPkj867tYi8) to register and pay online with PayPal by 8 PM the day before the program. There will be no refunds or credits.

We appreciate reservations by 8 PM the day before the program. Walk-ins may pay at the door on their mobile device.

**Membership in the AFduNS**
While no membership is required for newcomers, we welcome new members whose membership will support the activities of the AFduNS, a nonprofit 501(c)(3) organization. For further info, visit our [website](https://afnorthshore.org/events) or [click here to contact us](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScRxM03DpryO_lZlGWK-aDOCiFHFbivJimTScMOQmWk7KgOJQ/viewform?pli=1). If you wish to join the AFduNS, you may sign up [online](https://afnorthshore.org/join). Annual dues are $50.00.

title: Movie Night
description: Standing Above the Clouds From filmmaker Jalena Keane-Lee, this documentary shows the grassroots movement led by women from three Native Hawaiian families to stop a massive telescope from being built on the sacred mountain, Mauna Kea. title: The Founding Mothers with Laura Keyes
description: The “Mothers” of America aren’t often mentioned – the women whose influence on the early years of the United States reverberated for centuries afterwards. title: Morning Movie
description: A League of Their Own“There’s no crying in baseball!” Directed by Penny Marshall, this sports dramedy is a fictionalized story of the All-American Girls Professional Baseball League. It’s set in 1943 and focuses on The Rockford Peaches, an all-girl baseball team from Rockford, IL. They are a disparate group, with varying talents, and their manager Jimmy Dugan (Tom Hanks) is cynical and abrasive at first. However, the women are determined to make something of the team, to step out of stereotypical and traditional roles. Their enthusiasm has a positive effect on the manager. He becomes the manager the team needs and they, whether win or lose, know much was accomplished.  Starring Tom Hanks, Geena Davis, Rosie O'Donnell, Madonna.1992 : 128 minutes : PG for language: ComedyFree tickets will be given to those who attend.Watch the trailer for A League of Their Own.Find A League of Their Own in the library catalog.

title: Music Theater Works: Cats
description:
Music and Lyrics by Andrew Lloyd Webber
Based on “Old Possum’s Book of Practical Cats” by T.S. Eliot
Directed and Choreographed by Mandy Modic
Music Directed by Linda Madonia
Music Theater Works kicks off its 46th season with CATS, Andrew Lloyd Webber’s electrifying, Tony Award-winning musical. In this immersive production set in a larger-than-life British flat, audiences may join the magical and all encompassing world of the Jellicle cats as they gather for the annual Jellicle Ball, where one will be chosen for a new life. Through a series of dazzling musical numbers, each cat shares their story— from the mischievous Rum Tum Tugger, to the mysterious Macavity, and the once-glamorous Grizabella. Featuring unforgettable songs, breathtaking choreography, and a whimsical atmosphere, CATS is a celebration of life, community and the power of memory

title: Indie Lens Pop-Up: The Inquisitor
location: 6140 Lincoln Avenue, Morton Grove, IL, 60053, US
description: Explore the life and legacy of Barbara Jordan in The Inquisitor. Jordan was a groundbreaking Texas congresswoman whose sharp intellect and moral clarity transformed U.S. politics. From Nixon’s impeachment to civil rights battles, her voice demanded accountability, while she privately faced struggles few ever knew of. (2025, NR, 1hr 35min) About Indie Lens Pop-UpIndie Lens Pop-Up is a community series that brings people together for film screenings and conversations. Featuring documentaries seen on PBS's Independent Lens, Indie Lens Pop-Up draws local residents, leaders, and organizations to discuss what matters most. For more information, visit pbs.org/indielenspopup.

title: The Critique Group
description:
Evanston Writers Workshop
We have a new home! We will be meeting at the Art Maker's Outpost 609 South Blvd, Evanston, IL 60202. It is one block west and one block north of the South Blvd L station.

Welcome to the Evanston Writers Workshop Critique Group. One of two foundational programs of EWW, the Critique Group is a group of professional and aspiring writers who help each other polish their writing. Through workshop-style critique, writers have the opportunity to receive direct feedback from attentive readers who understand the craft of writing. For more information, weekly submission links, as well as submission guidelines and requirements, please visit [https://evanstonwritersworkshop.org/submissionguidlines](https://evanstonwritersworkshop.org/submissionguidlines) or our website at [www.evannstonwritersworkshop.org](www.evannstonwritersworkshop.org) .

Friendly reminder: please have your submissions uploaded by the Friday (midnight) before the meeting.

title: Current Politics with David Zarefsky
description:
A congregational favorite, this continuing lecture series is a discussion about contemporary public issues of interest to Jews as citizens.
David Zarefsky is the Owen L. Coon Professor Emeritus of Communication Studies at Northwestern University, specializing in argumentation and the analysis and criticism of American political discourse. He is a former president of the National Communication Association, the Rhetoric Society of America, and the Central States Communication Association. In 2012 he received the Lifetime Teaching Excellence Award from the National Communication Association. David is a past president of Beth Emet.
Members, register here.
Guests, register here.
Beth Emet uses budgeted funds and fees to support our adult education program. This year, instead of individual registration fees for specific classes, our goal is to secure $4,000 in donations to cover our costs. Please consider an optional donation to the adult education fund when you register to help sustain our commitment to lifelong learning.

The show also includes standout tracks from earlier albums and heartfelt tributes to classic blues legends. As one of today’s most dynamic performers, Joanne Shaw Taylor promises an unforgettable night of music.

title: Between the Lines: Sandwich by Catherine Newman
location: 6140 Lincoln Avenue, Morton Grove, IL, 60053, US
description: Between the Lines is now held on two different days, either in person or on Zoom. Titles are always available on Hoopla, as well as other library ebook platforms. New members are warmly welcome! Book Synopsis: From the beloved author of We All Want Impossible Things and Wreck, a moving, hilarious story of a family summer vacation full of secrets, lunch, and learning to let go.For the past two decades, Rocky has looked forward to her family’s yearly escape to Cape Cod. Their humble beach-town rental has been the site of sweet memories, sunny days, great meals, and messes of all kinds: emotional, marital, and—thanks to the cottage’s ancient plumbing—septic too.This year’s vacation, with Rocky sandwiched between her half-grown kids and fully aging parents, promises to be just as delightful as summers past—except, perhaps, for Rocky’s hormonal bouts of rage and melancholy. (Hello, menopause!) Her body is changing—her life is, too. And then a chain of events sends Rocky into the past, reliving both the tenderness and sorrow of a handful of long-ago summers.It's one precious week: everything is in balance; everything is in flux. And when Rocky comes face to face with her family’s history and future, she is forced to accept that she can no longer hide her secrets from the people she loves.

title: Beyond the Page with Percival Everett
description: Percival Everett, like his fiction, defies categorization. His most recent novel, James, earned both the Pulitzer Prize for fiction and the National Book Award, and his 2001 novel Erasure inspired the film American Fiction, which received the Academy Award for Best Adapted Screenplay in 2024. Everett’s other titles include Dr. No, The Trees (finalist for the Booker Prize and the PEN/Faulkner Award for Fiction), Telephone (finalist for the Pulitzer Prize), So Much Blue, and I Am Not Sidney Poitier. Despite these accomplishments, he remains devoted to reviewing his own work critically, and indeed his writing process involves intensive research and revision. Tune in for our conversation with Percival Everett to get a glimpse beyond the page. Brandis Friedman will moderate the event. She is a writer and anchor for WTTW’s Chicago Tonight and Chicago Tonight: Black Voices, and also serves as a champion for libraries.This event is made possible by Illinois Libraries Present (ILP), a statewide collaboration among public libraries offering premier events. ILP is funded in part by a grant awarded by the Illinois State Library, a department of the Office of Secretary of State, using funds provided by the U.S. Institute of Museum and Library Services, under the provisions of the Library Services and Technology Act. ILP is committed to inclusion and accessibility. To request accommodations, please email illinoislibrariespresent@ila.org. Register and receive a Zoom link for this event.

title: NSC Presents: Gregorian - Pure Chants World Tour
description:
VIP tickets include:
Premium Seat in the first 3 row.
Tote bag
Tour Laminate
CD
For 25 years, GREGORIAN have thrilled millions of people worldwide with their choreographed shows and their ability to switch seamlessly from classical chorales to pop and rock songs. Their concerts have set new standards.
With over 2,000 shows in 36 countries and more than 11 million albums sold, GREGORIAN are the most successful choir in the world.
In Spring 2026, GREGORIAN will continue with their PURE CHANTS World Tour in the USA.
Frank Peterson, the creator and producer of GREGORIAN says: “Our Pure Chants concerts bring the most popular GREGORIAN classics in an atmospheric and tranquil sound environment. Pure Chants concerts are extremely peaceful, yet spectacular and unite the audience with the most beautiful melodies in the world.”
The idea for GREGORIAN was born when Frank Peterson visited the royal monastery of San Lorenzo de El Escorial in Spain in 1989. Wandering through the medieval monastery and castle complex and listening to contemporary music on his Walkman, he found the inspiration for the sound experiment of mixing ancient Gregorian music with modern music.
Peterson first realized his idea with Michael Cretu and the worldwide sound phenomenon “Enigma”. Their first album “MCMXC a.D.” was released in 1990 and quickly became a worldwide success.
“MCMXC a.D.” was number 1 in 41 different countries and went 57 times platinum and 45 times gold. It is considered as the most successful German album of all time worldwide.
This gigantic success paved the way for GREGORIAN to take the musical idea one step further and to establish it as an independent music act. In 1999, with the start of production on the first album “Masters Of Chant”, Frank Peterson seamlessly built on the success of “Enigma”.
In the following years, GREGORIAN has sold over 11 million CDs and 7 DVDs worldwide and achieved gold and platinum status in 24 different countries.
At the end of 2024 and thus in time for their Anniversary World Tour in 2025, Frank Peterson released a new GREGORIAN album. The “25/2025” album includes new songs as well as re-recordings of some well-known GREGORIAN classics.
Frank Peterson has been involved in various musical projects and international productions for over 30 years. He has worked as a producer with Sarah Brightman, Enigma, Justin Timberlake, Andrea Bocelli, Paul Stanley (Kiss), Backstreet Boys, Alannah Myles, José Carreras, Tom Jones, Plácido Domingo, Errol Brown, Ofra Haza, Sandra, Josh Groban, Marky Mark, I Muvrini, Florent Pagny and Ricardo Cocciante, among others.
In addition to GREGORIAN, Peterson has also been working as a show producer for Sarah Brightman and other top stars since 2005. Together with the world star, he produced the album and cinema production “Sarah Brightman – Hymn”, which was acclaimed by critics worldwide.

title: Friday Film
description: A Beautiful Day in the NeighborhoodTom Hanks received an Oscar nomination for playing Fred Rogers, creator of the beloved TV show, Mister Rogers' Neighborhood. The story follows Lloyd Vogel, a cynical reporter assigned to write a profile on Fred Rogers for a magazine article about heroes. He has a reputation for being hard on his subjects and holds much anger toward life in general, his father in particular. His interviews with Fred Rogers have a profound effect on his outlook and help him to learn how to forgive. Starring Starring Tom Hanks, Matthew Rhys, Susan Kelechi Watson, and Chris Cooper.2019 : 109 minutes : Rated PG for some strong thematic material, a brief fight, and some mild language: DramaFree tickets will be given to those who attend.Watch a trailer for A Beautiful Day in the Neighborhood.Find A Beautiful Day in the Neighborhood in the library catalog.

title: Music Theater Works: Cats
description:
Music and Lyrics by Andrew Lloyd Webber
Based on “Old Possum’s Book of Practical Cats” by T.S. Eliot
Directed and Choreographed by Mandy Modic
Music Directed by Linda Madonia
Music Theater Works kicks off its 46th season with CATS, Andrew Lloyd Webber’s electrifying, Tony Award-winning musical. In this immersive production set in a larger-than-life British flat, audiences may join the magical and all encompassing world of the Jellicle cats as they gather for the annual Jellicle Ball, where one will be chosen for a new life. Through a series of dazzling musical numbers, each cat shares their story— from the mischievous Rum Tum Tugger, to the mysterious Macavity, and the once-glamorous Grizabella. Featuring unforgettable songs, breathtaking choreography, and a whimsical atmosphere, CATS is a celebration of life, community and the power of memory

title: NSC & Chicago Philharmonic Orchestra Present: Dirty Dancing in Concert
description:
You fell in love with their first dance, now it’s your turn to relive it.
Dirty Dancing in Concert is a cinematic live-to-film concert event that brings the iconic love story back to the big screen, with a twist.
Watch the digitally remastered film in full on a massive cinema screen, while a live band and singers perform every unforgettable song from the original soundtrack, syncing with each moment of the movie.
As the story of Baby and Johnny unfolds, you’ll sing, sway, and relive the time of your life, surrounded by fellow fans who love the film just as much as you do.
But the magic doesn’t stop when the credits roll. After the film, the celebration continues with an encore party where you’ll be invited to sing and dance along to the hits that defined a generation.
This is more than a concert, it’s a love letter to the music, the moves, and the moment that changed everything.
Whether you’re reliving it or discovering it for the first time, one thing’s for sure: Some dances last forever.

title: 3/22 @ 1PM - Sunday Games at Sketchbook Evanston
description:
Evanston Board Games Meetup
Join us at Sketchbook Brewing Company in Evanston for our Sunday Board Game Meetup!

We meet most Sundays at Sketchbook. Just look for the group in the back that has stacks of board games to pick from! We welcome people of all skill levels to come and play, as we always teach new games to people that have not played them before.

Feel free to bring your own games that you want to teach and are familiar with. A typical event starts with talking about what we brought that week, and then we break out into smaller groups to play games based on player count and what everyone is interested in. It's common to have 2 or 3 games going at once, and fitting in a second or third game before the event ends at 4pm. Please RSVP and be on time so that we can include you at the start of the first game, and buy a drink to support the venue!

title: Meet Me in a Moment
description: Enjoy the music of four great Jewish composers and learn about their lives and works. Each musical selection for violin and piano is followed by a historically informed, theatrical scene from the composer's life. Written and directed by Aviva Chertok Li, this one-of-a-kind production will bring you the best of Jewish classical music while immersing you in Jewish history and culture.Step back in time to Cleveland of the 1920’s and the living room of composer Ernest Bloch, join a rehearsal at the Jerusalem Academy of Music with Paul Ben-Haim, and attend a meeting of the Society of Jewish Folk Music in St. Petersburg with Lazare Saminsky. Meet one of America’s greatest modern Jewish composers, through a special video screening of violinist Aviva Chertok Li's interview with Grammy-nominated composer, Jonathan Leshnoff. Free tickets will be given to those present starting at 2:30 pm.

title: Solstice: A Winter Circus Experience
description:
Step into a dreamscape with “Solstice: a Winter Circus Experience” by Circus EVO. At the heart of the story is a girl who loses her light and stumbles into a world where time bends, memories breathe, and the familiar becomes impossibly strange. Shadows dance, snow drifts fall, and acrobats blur the line between the everyday and the extraordinary. More than a circus, “Solstice” is a living poem — a place where nostalgia meets surreal wonder, and where audiences are invited to rediscover the childlike awe of seeing snow for the first time!
In 2026, Solstice returns with all-new elements and an original score composed by NC artist Bella Nona, weaving music, movement, and imagination into a one-of-a-kind celebration of the human spirit. Don’t Miss it!

About Circus EVO:
Circus EVO is an International Circus Company based in North Carolina. Since 2020 we’ve transformed urban spaces into vibrant stages, bridging the gap between art and community through Contemporary Circus, Dance, Music, Theater, and more! Our Mission to EVOke Emotions and EVOlve Circus Art empowers Artists of all backgrounds, while bringing a new way to entertain and provide cultural value to our community.
Over the past 5 years, we have emerged as a dynamic force, dedicated to delivering high-quality, multi-disciplinary performances. Since our inception, Circus EVO has performed over 70 times in 11 cities for thousands of people, supporting more than 70 artists and creatives along the way.

title: Monday Matinees
location: 4000 W. Pratt Ave., Lincolnwood, IL, 60712, US
description: Enjoy great films at your library every Monday! Doors open at 12:45pm.  Father of the Bride (1991)Mon, Mar 2 • 1-2:45pm • PG, 100 mins •  [D]In this remake of the 1950 comedy, a frazzled shoe executive tries to cope with the financial and emotional costs of his only daughter's impending marriage while also dealing with an eccentric wedding coordinator. **Paulie (1998)Mon, Mar 9 • 1-2:45pm • PG, 91 mins •  [D]A chatty parrot named Paulie recalls his search to find the little girl who first owned him and gets help from a kind janitor. Wicked: For Good (2025)Mon, Mar 23 • 1-3:30pm • PG, 138 mins •  [D]Now demonized as the Wicked Witch of the West, Elphaba lives in exile in the Ozian forest, while Glinda resides at the palace in Emerald City, reveling in the perks of fame and popularity. As an angry mob rises against the Wicked Witch, she'll need to reunite with Glinda to transform herself, and all of Oz, for good. The Long Walk (2025)Mon, Mar 30 • 1-3pm • R, 108 mins •  [D]In a dystopian near-future, 100 teenage boys must embark on an annual competitive test known as "The Long Walk." The rules are simple: maintain a speed above 4 miles per hour. Receive three warnings in an hour and you're shot dead. The last one walking gets whatever he wants for the rest of his life. Under these grim circumstances, the boys develop deep friendships despite knowing that each of their friends' survival is a threat to their own. There will be no Monday Matinee on March 16 due to the library serving as a polling location.

title: We The Native People
description: How did the signing of the Declaration of Independence affect the Native American people? How did the government and its people view Native Americans, and what were the consequences of those views? Kim Sigafus will discuss the impact the Declaration of Independence had on the lives of the Native people and what ultimately was lost. She will cover how the Native people once lived, worked the land, and raised their children, and explain what was lost of their culture as western expansion took place. You will have the chance to learn music, drum, sing, and play Native American instruments. Traditional foods will also be discussed, and a recipe will be handed out. Kim Sigafus is an award-winning Ojibwa author who writes Native American fiction, nonfiction, children’s, and young adult books. She has presented on Native history, food, and music, and has traveled all over the Midwest in her native regalia, bringing Native culture to life. Kim’s family is from White Earth Reservation, located in the northwest corner of Minnesota.Keyword: USA 250

title: *RESCHEDULED* Words That Change Lives: A Conversation with Chicago Books to Women in Prison (R)
location: 4000 W. Pratt Ave., Lincolnwood, IL, 60712, US
description: Join us for an inspiring evening that explores the power of books to heal, connect, and transform lives. In partnership with Chicago Books to Women in Prison, we’ll learn how access to reading supports education, reflection, escape, and much more for women and trans people who are incarcerated. Volunteers from the organization will share their experiences sending books to readers behind bars, and we’ll hear from someone who will share how the program supported them during their time in prison and beyond.

title: Abigail Adams: A Dramatic Portrayal by Leslie Goddard
description: More than just a wife of 2nd president John Adams and mother of 6th president John Quincy Adams, Abigail Adams was a true partner to her husband and a committed patriot. In this living-history portrayal by Leslie Goddard, Adams recounts her life, including her famous call to John Adams to "remember the Ladies" in the founding of the new nation. She shares her opinions of the founding fathers, displays her lively wit and offers a glimpse at one of the great love stories of all time.

Meet fellow Francophones for evening conversation. This gathering is facilitated by one or more of our native or fluent AFduNS leaders.

Pour yourself a glass of wine, gather some snacks and settle in at home for our online conversation group.

The meeting will be held on Zoom. Although you don't need to download Zoom to attend this meeting, we encourage you to do so (https://zoom.us/download) for a smoother experience. Depending on the number of attendees, we may break out into smaller groups.

The link to the meeting will be visible when you RSVP. The meeting ID is 859 8255 4800.

All fluency levels are welcome. RSVPs help us plan.

**Membership in the AFduNS**
While no membership is required for newcomers, we welcome new members whose membership will support the activities of the AFduNS, a nonprofit 501(c)(3) organization. For further info, visit our [website](https://afnorthshore.org/events) or [click here to contact us](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScRxM03DpryO_lZlGWK-aDOCiFHFbivJimTScMOQmWk7KgOJQ/viewform?pli=1). title: A Magician Among the Spirits- Houdini and the Supernatural
description: Before his death, Houdini stated that should it be possible to contact the living from the other side, he would do so. The question remains as to whether or not he actually succeeded…Join Troy Taylor on what would have been Houdini's 152nd birthday (100 years after his death) to learn about this American enigma's life, and possible afterlife.

title: Between the Lines: Sandwich by Catherine Newman (Online)
description: Between the Lines meets for online discussions via Zoom. Registered patrons will receive an email the day before the program with the meeting ID and password for joining the event. New members are warmly welcome!Titles are always available on Hoopla, as well as other library ebook platforms. Contact us for assistance, or find any instructions you may need on our Download & Stream page.Book Synopsis: From the beloved author of We All Want Impossible Things and Wreck, a moving, hilarious story of a family summer vacation full of secrets, lunch, and learning to let go.For the past two decades, Rocky has looked forward to her family’s yearly escape to Cape Cod. Their humble beach-town rental has been the site of sweet memories, sunny days, great meals, and messes of all kinds: emotional, marital, and—thanks to the cottage’s ancient plumbing—septic too.This year’s vacation, with Rocky sandwiched between her half-grown kids and fully aging parents, promises to be just as delightful as summers past—except, perhaps, for Rocky’s hormonal bouts of rage and melancholy. (Hello, menopause!) Her body is changing—her life is, too. And then a chain of events sends Rocky into the past, reliving both the tenderness and sorrow of a handful of long-ago summers.It's one precious week: everything is in balance; everything is in flux. And when Rocky comes face to face with her family’s history and future, she is forced to accept that she can no longer hide her secrets from the people she loves.

title: Fantasy Book Club
description: This month we'll be discussing The Jasad Heir by Sara Hashem. Silvia, the Heir of Jasad, should not have survived the destruction of her family, but she fled her kingdom and spent the next decade in hiding. Forbidden magic flows through her veins, and those in power raze cities in order to extinguish it. When the enemy Nizhali prince discovers her magic, he strikes her a bargain: be his champion in the deadly Alcalah tournament. Win for his kingdom, and she can earn her freedom. This is the first book in Sara Hashem's completed romantasy duology, which is set in an Egyptian-inspired fantasy world.Find The Jasad Heir in the library catalog.

title: Online Fiction Critique Group
description:
Evanston Writers Workshop
Welcome to the Evanston Writers Workshop Online Critique Group. We're a group of professional and aspiring writers who help each other polish our writing through workshop style critique. Writers have the opportunity to receive and give direct, constructive, and actionable feedback from attentive readers and writers who understand the craft of writing.

We will critique 2-3 submissions (maximum 3,000 words each) per meeting.
You do not need to be a member to attend or critique, however you need to be a member in order to submit and have your own work critiqued.
If you have a submission, please submit it via this link:
https://form.jotform.com/251125391424147
by midnight the Friday before critique meeting.

We will meet monthly on the fourth Tuesday of the month. Although we've been critiquing in-person since 2007, these are our first online meetings. Please visit the EWW website (https://evanstonwritersworkshop.org/#calendar-and-submission-links) to view current submissions.

Fiction writers of all levels of all genres are welcome, ages 18+.

For more information, submission guidelines and requirements, please visit the Evanston Writers Workshop message board here evanstonwritersworkshop.org. Or you can direct questions to raven.reynolds.author@gmail.com.

Friendly reminder: please send your submissions by the Friday (midnight) before the meeting.

EWW is a not-for-profit 501 (C) 3 organization founded in 2007.

title: Heart of a Dog
description:
CHERRY ORCHARD FESTIVAL PRESENTS
HEART OF A DOG
Based on the satirical novel by Mikhail Bulgakov
Directed by Konstantin Kamensky
Starring: Alexander Feklistov, Artur Smolyaninov, Semyon Shteinberg, Grigory Sluzhitel, Sasha Aleksis and Irina Soboleva
Tuesday, March 24 8:00 PM
North Shore Center for the Performing Arts
9501 Skokie Blvd, Skokie, IL
Step into the surreal and satirical world of Mikhail Bulgakov’s Heart of a Dog, where a stray dog’s astonishing transformation into a man reveals the absurdities, contradictions, and fragile hopes of a society in flux. Sharp humor meets profound social commentary in this gripping tale, making Bulgakov’s classic as electrifying and relevant today as it was in 1920s Moscow.
Under the visionary direction of Konstantin Kamensky, the production comes alive with immersive multimedia, striking visuals, and bold performances.
More than a play, Heart of a Dog is an unforgettable theatrical journey that entertains, challenges, and lingers long after the curtain falls—holding a mirror up to our world with Bulgakov’s timeless wit and piercing insight.
Duration: 1 hour 45 minutes (no intermission)
This play is performed in Russian without subtitles

title: Music Theater Works: Cats
description:
Music and Lyrics by Andrew Lloyd Webber
Based on “Old Possum’s Book of Practical Cats” by T.S. Eliot
Directed and Choreographed by Mandy Modic
Music Directed by Linda Madonia
Music Theater Works kicks off its 46th season with CATS, Andrew Lloyd Webber’s electrifying, Tony Award-winning musical. In this immersive production set in a larger-than-life British flat, audiences may join the magical and all encompassing world of the Jellicle cats as they gather for the annual Jellicle Ball, where one will be chosen for a new life. Through a series of dazzling musical numbers, each cat shares their story— from the mischievous Rum Tum Tugger, to the mysterious Macavity, and the once-glamorous Grizabella. Featuring unforgettable songs, breathtaking choreography, and a whimsical atmosphere, CATS is a celebration of life, community and the power of memory

title: Evanston Writers’ Workshop Long Development Group--meeting
description:
Evanston Writers Workshop
Howdy all,

Congratulations to Marcia Harris – the newest submission to the Evanston Writers’ Workshop Long Development Group.

Long Development Group mission blurb is to provide feedback and critique of a longer, more developed piece of 10,000 to 30,000 words. To remind those interested in participating: the goal of this group is to analyze and give more overview-type feedback (e.g., plot, premise, structure, consistent voice, etc.)

\*new\*
Authors are encouraged to write a full summary of their submissions/project’s plot, this can be abstracted bullet points if needed. (circa 500 words.)
Feel to include the mission of the piece. What is the X, Y and Z of this story? Feel free to include tropes, inspirations, or the ontological motivations for the why this story exists. (circa 500 words. summary/motivations do not count against the 30,000 word limit.)

Please include all questions that you would like to be answered. What do you want us to read for? What do you think is a particular weakness of the work, are you trying to develop a subplot, any motifs, things like that. Maximum 20 questions, try to make the questions as in-depth as possible.

--For those who wish to read it and comment on Marcia's submission (whether or not you are able to come to the meeting), you can access it here:
https://docs.google.com/document/d/15ZOuWzwnYDFPOYYawmpbfnO3e83To3vM/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=100821020442953994836&rtpof=true&sd=true

Our in-person discussion of Marcia's piece will
Meeting location and time March the 26th, 7:00 p.m. In the lobby of the Orrington Hotel, 1710 Orrington Avenue.

We look forward to your joining the nest Developmental Group meeting!

Featuring unforgettable songs, breathtaking choreography, and a whimsical atmosphere, CATS is a celebration of life, community and the power of memory

title: North Shore Center Presents: Talib Kweli
description:
The Brooklyn-based MC earned his stripes as one of the most lyrically-gifted, socially aware and politically insightful rappers to emerge in the last 20 years.
“I’m a touring artist. I’m an artist that’s internationally known. I’m not just a local artist at this point in my career. I’m cognizant of the fact that what I do is beyond where it started. I’m trying to reach the apex of where I am now, but without turning my back on or dismissing what I’ve done before.”
After nearly 20 years of releasing mesmerizing music, Talib Kweli stands as one of the world’s most talented and most accomplished Hip Hop artists. Whether working with Mos Def as one-half of Black Star, partnering with producer Hi-Tek for Reflection Eternal, releasing landmark solo material or collaborating with Kanye West, Pharrell Williams, Just Blaze, J Dilla, or Madlib, Kweli commands attention by delivering top-tier lyricism, crafting captivating stories and showing the ability to rhyme over virtually any type of instrumental.
In 2011, Kweli founded Javotti Media, which is self-defined as “a platform for independent thinkers and doers.” Kweli has set out to make Javotti Media (which released his 2011 album, Gutter Rainbows, and is named after his paternal grandmother) into a media powerhouse that releases music, films and books.

title: 3/29 @ 1PM - Sunday Games at Sketchbook Evanston
description:
Evanston Board Games Meetup
Join us at Sketchbook Brewing Company in Evanston for our Sunday Board Game Meetup!

We meet most Sundays at Sketchbook. Just look for the group in the back that has stacks of board games to pick from! We welcome people of all skill levels to come and play, as we always teach new games to people that have not played them before.

Feel free to bring your own games that you want to teach and are familiar with. A typical event starts with talking about what we brought that week, and then we break out into smaller groups to play games based on player count and what everyone is interested in. It's common to have 2 or 3 games going at once, and fitting in a second or third game before the event ends at 4pm. Please RSVP and be on time so that we can include you at the start of the first game, and buy a drink to support the venue!

title: #113: The Seven Daughters of Dupree by Nikesha Elise Williams
description:
Black Girls Read Book Club
**Our March book selection is *The Seven Daughters of Dupree* by Chicago native Nikesha Elise Williams!** We will meet at [Zora's Place](https://www.zorasplace.com/), Evanston's only Black woman-owned bookshop.

About the book: It’s 1995, and fourteen-year-old Tati is determined to uncover the identity of her father. But her mother, Nadia, keeps her secrets close, while her grandmother Gladys remains silent about the family’s past, including why she left Land’s End, Alabama, in 1953. As Tati digs deeper, she uncovers a legacy of family secrets, where every generation of Dupree women has posed more questions than answers.

From Jubi in 1917, whose attempt to pass for white ends when she gives birth to Ruby; to Ruby’s fiery lust for Sampson in 1934 that leads to a baby of her own; to the night in 1980 that changed Nadia’s future forever, the Dupree women carry the weight of their heritage. Bound by a mysterious malediction that means they will only give birth to daughters, the Dupree women confront a legacy of pain, resilience, and survival that began with an enslaved ancestor who risked everything for freedom.

*The Seven Daughters of Dupree* masterfully weaves together themes of generational trauma, Black women’s resilience, and unbreakable familial bonds. Nikesha Elise Williams delivers a feminist literary fiction that explores the ripple effects of actions, secrets, and love through seven generations of Black women.

***The Seven Daughters of Dupree* will be released in January 2026 and is available for pre-order now. Support Zora's Place by purchasing your book at [their brick-and-mortar storefront](https://www.zorasplace.com/) or [their online storefront on Bookshop](https://bookshop.org/a/5326/9781668051948).**

title: Monday Matinees
location: 4000 W. Pratt Ave., Lincolnwood, IL, 60712, US
description: Enjoy great films at your library every Monday! Doors open at 12:45pm.  Father of the Bride (1991)Mon, Mar 2 • 1-2:45pm • PG, 100 mins •  [D]In this remake of the 1950 comedy, a frazzled shoe executive tries to cope with the financial and emotional costs of his only daughter's impending marriage while also dealing with an eccentric wedding coordinator. **Paulie (1998)Mon, Mar 9 • 1-2:45pm • PG, 91 mins •  [D]A chatty parrot named Paulie recalls his search to find the little girl who first owned him and gets help from a kind janitor. Wicked: For Good (2025)Mon, Mar 23 • 1-3:30pm • PG, 138 mins •  [D]Now demonized as the Wicked Witch of the West, Elphaba lives in exile in the Ozian forest, while Glinda resides at the palace in Emerald City, reveling in the perks of fame and popularity. As an angry mob rises against the Wicked Witch, she'll need to reunite with Glinda to transform herself, and all of Oz, for good. The Long Walk (2025)Mon, Mar 30 • 1-3pm • R, 108 mins •  [D]In a dystopian near-future, 100 teenage boys must embark on an annual competitive test known as "The Long Walk." The rules are simple: maintain a speed above 4 miles per hour. Receive three warnings in an hour and you're shot dead. The last one walking gets whatever he wants for the rest of his life. Under these grim circumstances, the boys develop deep friendships despite knowing that each of their friends' survival is a threat to their own. There will be no Monday Matinee on March 16 due to the library serving as a polling location.

title: Introduction to News Media Literacy
description: Our media ecosystem today is increasingly messy. It's a mix of professionally produced and user-generated content that often blurs together on platforms like YouTube, Facebook, and TikTok. The rapid rise of artificial intelligence has added yet another layer of complexity, making it even easier for misleading or fabricated material to appear credible. Michael A. Spikes of Northwestern University’s Medill School of Journalism explores how this blending of sources—and now AI-generated material—creates a “blurring of the lines” between types of content, making it harder to know what to trust. You'll gain practical skills for assessing the credibility of information from different outlets and strategies for building a set of reliable sources you can return to regularly.Michael Spikes, Ph.D., has been teaching, writing about, and developing curriculum on news media literacy for more than 15 years. Currently, he is a professor of practice and the program director of Teach for Chicago Journalism at the Medill School of Journalism at Northwestern University. Before joining Northwestern, Michael was a project manager for the Center for News Literacy at Stony Brook University. This event is part of the Civic Empowerment Series 2026, in partnership with Schaumburg Township District Library, Vernon Area Public Library, Blue Island Public Library, Highland Park Public Library, Aurora Public Library District, La Grange Public Library, Fox River Valley Public Library, Algonquin Area Public Library District, and Oak Park Public Library. Register and receive a Zoom link for this event.For questions about this event, call Schaumburg Township District Library at 847-923-3347.

title: Navigating News Media with Michael Spikes [Z]
description: Join us for a virtual session of the Civic Empowerment Series 2026, presented in partnership with Schaumburg Public Library and other area libraries. Dr. Michael A. Spikes of Northwestern University’s Medill School of Journalism will explain how today’s media landscape—shaped by social platforms, mixed information sources, and AI-generated content—can make it difficult to know what to trust. Learn practical strategies for evaluating credibility and building a reliable set of news sources you can use with confidence.The Civic Empowerment Series 2026 is brought to you in partnership with Schaumburg Public Library, Skokie Public Library, Vernon Area Public Library, Blue Island Public Library, Highland Park Public Library, Aurora Public Library District, La Grange Public Library, Fox River Valley Public Library, Algonquin Area Public Library District, Roselle Public Library and Oak Park Public Library. Please note: On the Schaumburg Township District Library registration page, please select online. Schaumburg Township District Library will email a Zoom link three hours prior to the start of the program.This program will take place on Zoom.Register Here!

title: The Alamo and The Republic of Tejas
description: This presentation reexamines the Alamo and the Republic of Tejas, cutting through familiar myths with historical context and turning a sharp eye toward American legend-making. It explores how rebellion, expansion, and selective memory shaped nationalism, borders, and Texan culture—often at Mexico’s expense. The Alamo, The Republic of Tejas, and Never Forgetting invites audiences to remember the past a little less comfortably, and a lot more honestly.

title: Religious Studies Speaker Series: Brett Hendrickson
description:
Miraculous Earth: The Santuario de Chimayó

Northern New Mexico boasts dozens of colonial-era adobe churches, most built by Pueblo and Hispano hands under the watchful guidance of Spanish Franciscan friars. Each is special in its own way, but one stands out among the rest: the Santuario de Chimayó, located in the village of Chimayó. What makes this so unique? In one of its side chapels, there is a hole in the stone floor, opening onto the sandy earth. Hundreds of thousands of pilgrims, tourists, and other visitors come to the Santuario every year to gather dirt from this hole, many believing it has miraculous healing properties. Sometimes called the “Lourdes of America,” the Santuario is one of the largest sites of Catholic pilgrimage in North America and a monument to the Hispanic and Catholic origins of much of what is modern United States. In this anecdote-rich talk about the Santuario, Prof. Brett Hendrickson will discuss the various political and religious regimes that have ruled New Mexico, examine the meaning of miracles in Hispanic Catholic contexts, and suggest that our nation’s origin stories don’t just start on the East Coast but also belong in the U.S.-Mexico borderlands.

Please register to attend.

title: The Critique Group
description:
Evanston Writers Workshop
We have a new home! We will be meeting at the Art Maker's Outpost 609 South Blvd, Evanston, IL 60202. It is one block west and one block north of the South Blvd L station.

Welcome to the Evanston Writers Workshop Critique Group. One of two foundational programs of EWW, the Critique Group is a group of professional and aspiring writers who help each other polish their writing. Through workshop-style critique, writers have the opportunity to receive direct feedback from attentive readers who understand the craft of writing. For more information, weekly submission links, as well as submission guidelines and requirements, please visit the Evanston Writers Workshop message board here [https://www.meetup.com/evanstonwriters/messages/boards/](https://www.meetup.com/evanstonwriters/messages/boards/) or our website at [www.evannstonwritersworkshop.org](www.evannstonwritersworkshop.org) .

Friendly reminder: please have your submissions uploaded by the Friday (midnight) before the meeting.

title: Honors Program Speaker: Patrick Hassan
description:
Iran has a long history of foreign interference in its domestic affairs. Arguably, this context of foreign interference has been the primary force shaping how the ruling body of the Islamic Republic of Iran purportedly conceives of itself and its governing principles, namely: as the sole entity capable of upholding Iran's national sovereignty in the face of foreign (i.e., Western) domination. From a realist perspective, this talk explores how the Islamic Republic's projected anti-imperialist stance—something increasingly repeated by some portions of the Western Left—might be best understood and evaluated in light of its demonstrated practical aims, and especially the means it has frequently taken in pursuit of those aims (e.g., severe domestic repression; proxy warfare). By doing so, I aim to shed light on two broader philosophical themes potentially instructive beyond the Iranian case: (a) how and why hawkish or liberal-interventionist strategies can be self-undermining; (b) how and why ideological ‘camp’ thinking and selective solidarity can produce dangerous double standards among otherwise well-meaning anti-imperialists.

Please register to attend.

title: Music Monday
description: Chicago plays the blues a little bit differently. When the blues performers came from the Mississippi Delta to the packed Chicago jazz clubs, they had to adapt their music to be heard, and they created the iconic and influential style we hear today. Learn about some favorite Chicago blues musicians, the history of the Chicago blues, and how it changed the music that came after.Each Music Monday event includes a presentation related to music history, followed by discussion.

title: Open Playtime
description: Join us for open-ended play! We will have a variety of toys available for your little one to play with.

title: ELL Literacy Circle
description: Have you ever wanted to be part of a library book discussion group, but you worried that your English language skills were not strong enough?In this class for advanced-level English learners, we will read and discuss an American novel together, with the support of Margaret Feit Clarke, an experienced ESL teacher from the ELL Parent Center. You will strengthen your English skills while learning more about great literature, American culture, and the Skokie community. Limited to 16 participants. Free tickets will be given to those who attend.The class will meet on Tuesdays for 12 weeks: March 3-May 12, 9:30-11:30 am. No class March 31.We’ll read and talk about Olive, Again by Elizabeth Strout. The book offers a series of interconnected stories that provide a portrait of a complicated woman confronting her own mortality.Find Olive, Again in the library catalog.Find additional titles by Elizabeth Strout in the library catalog.

Stick around for informal discussion and food. For grade 8-12. Free tickets will be given to those who attend.Turning Point Behavioral Health is an outpatient mental health center with a mission to provide solid support when and where you need it most. This event is supported by the Skokie Community Foundation and The Education Foundation.

location: 4000 W. Pratt Ave., Lincolnwood, IL, 60712, US
description: Bring a bag of your favorite sour candy to swap with fellow sour candy aficionados. Discover new favorites and taste a curated selection of the finest sour candies, handpicked by the sour candy experts at Lincolnwood Library. Candy is not required for attendance.GRADES 6-12

title: Introduction to Tinkercad
description: Discover the basics of 3D design in this introductory class using Tinkercad, free and user-friendly software for creating 3D prints. In this one-hour session, you will learn the basics of 3D modeling, navigating the platform, and building simple models. You will pick up helpful tips and tricks to design your own project. Beginners welcome--no experience required. After the class, you are welcome to finish your designs to submit them for 3D printing. Keyword: Consumer Information

title: Skokie Show-and-Tell
description: What’s your Skokie story? Bring a Skokie-related object or photograph to show and tell your story or just come to listen. We will capture memories and stories of Skokie as part of the Skokie Yearbook Project. Free tickets will be given to all who attend.

title: Making the Most of Social Security (R)
location: 4000 W. Pratt Ave., Lincolnwood, IL, 60712, US
description: Do you want to get the most out of your Social Security? Discover strategies to boost your monthly lifetime benefits beyond standard filing options. This seminar from The Dala Group offers tailored guidance for singles, married couples, divorced individuals, and widows. You’ll also learn about earned income limits and how Social Security benefits are taxed; key information to help you make informed retirement decisions.

title: The Happy Invention Postcard Show and Tell
description: Katherine Hamilton-Smith, founding curator of the Curt Teich Postcard Archives Collection, now at the Newberry Library in Chicago, returns to the Winnetka-Northfield Library to reprise her lavishly-illustrated presentation on the history and significance of American picture postcards. Attendees are encouraged to bring their own family postcards for comment on their historic and collectible value.

description: This lecture, as part of the Jewish Studies Speaker Series, examines President Harry S Truman and Civil Rights, including his landmark desegregation of the U.S. military and its lasting impact, with historical context and insights from Cassie Pikarsky, Director of Strategic Initiatives for the Truman Library Institute. Please register to attend.

title: Handwriting Analysis
description: Graphologist Lorrisa Juliano will explain what the size of your words, margins and spacing of your writing says about you and your relationships! She will share celebrity handwriting samples, stories and insights about what handwriting reveals about all of us.

title: Myths in American History
description: All countries tweak their national stories to make the past easier to explain. But the reality is often far more interesting than the myth. Historian Joyce Haworth will explore a collection of these stories, from the light-hearted (was Molly Pitcher a real person?) to the serious (did Lincoln hold enslaved labor?). She will also provide some help in learning how to detect the fictions within the truth.Keyword: USA 250

title: LitLounge
description: This month we will be discussing The Book of Hope by Jane Goodall.Naturalist Jane Goodall's last written work is an inspiring and reflective piece that offers advice on how we can hold on to hope in a world that seems so troubled. Presented in partnership with Morton Grove Public Library.Find The Book of Hope in our catalog.Keyword: Book Lovers

title: LitLounge: The Book of Hope By Jane Goodall
location: Cosmos Room, 5215 Oakton Street, Skokie, IL, 60077, US
description: LitLounge is meeting in partnership with Skokie Public Library. Contact Cailyn Heintzelman (cheintzelman@mgpl.org) for details.This meeting is being held at the Skokie Public Library, in the Cosmos Room.The book can be checked out through all library ebook platforms, including Hoopla where copies are always available. Find instructions on our Download & Stream page, or contact us for assistance.Book Synopsis: A famous naturalist and a best-selling author explore how do we hold on to hope in a world that seems so troubled.

This event is designed for TRIO students. For questions and additional information about this college visit, please email TRIO at trio@oakton.edu.

title: Ciné Club
description:
Alliance Française du North Shore
Join a group of *cinéphiles* online for a discussion in French of a French film with English subtitles.

The choice for this month is *Jules et Jim*. You'll watch the film on your own: view it for free on [Kanopy](https://www.kanopy.com/en/). For more details, write to cine-club@AFnorthshore.org.

**Membership in the AFduNS**
While no membership is required for newcomers, we welcome new members whose membership will support the activities of the AFduNS, a nonprofit 501(c)(3) organization. For further info, visit our [website](https://afnorthshore.org/) or [contact us](https://afnorthshore.org/contact-us). If you wish to join the AFduNS, you may sign up [online](https://afnorthshore.org/join). Annual dues are $50.00.

For grade 6-12.

title: Citizenship Interview Practice and Immigration Information
description: Drop in for one-on-one practice to help you prepare for your citizenship interview.During the actual citizenship interview, an immigration officer will ask you about your application and background. You will also need to take an English and civics test.Please bring your completed N-400 application if you have one. Stop by HIAS Immigration & Citizenship’s information table, where you can learn about resources and ask questions on a wide range of immigration topics.All information shared will be confidential.

title: Board of Trustees Meeting
location: 6140 Lincoln Avenue, Morton Grove, IL, 60053, US
description: The Board of Trustees meets on the second Thursday of each month. The public is welcome to attend the meetings. Learn more about the Board of Trustees.PLEASE NOTE: To have a public comment read into the minutes, please contact Executive Director Pam Leffler at pleffler@mgpl.org.

title: Budgeting and Saving
description: Ryan Decker, Associate Professor of Economics; Director for the Center for Financial Literacy; North Central College, breaks down the basics of personal finance: budgeting and introductory saving and investment strategies. Learn which factors to consider for deciding when, where, and how to save, invest, or aggressively tackle debt. Resources and tools for learning more about these topics will be shared.This is one of a collaborative series of virtual events presented by BIG (Business Interest Group) and co-sponsored by 23 public libraries in the Chicago area for Financial Fitness 2026.Register for this webinar here:https://us02web.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_5oXcK8R7S0OoV97Z4VJ2FA

title: Budgeting and Saving Basics
description: Learn about the basics of personal finance, budgeting, and introductory saving and investment strategies, including factors in deciding when, where, and how to save, invest, or tackle debt. Resources and tools for learning more about these topics will be shared.Dr. Ryan Decker, CFP is a Professor of Finance at North Central College where he serves as the Founding Director of the Center for Financial Literacy. Dr. Decker runs financial education programs for high schools, colleges, nonprofit organizations, and corporations.This webinar is part of Financial Fitness 2026, a free financial literacy series presented by BIG (Business Interest Group), comprised of business librarians in the Chicago suburbs. These virtual programs offer practical tips and advice from experienced financial advisors.Register here to receive a Zoom link.Keyword: Consumer Information

title: Financial Fitness Series: Budgeting and Savings Basics (Online)
description: Ryan Decker breaks down the basics of personal finance: budgeting, introductory saving, and investment strategies. He will also share tools and resources for learning more about these topics.Register hereFinancial Fitness 2026 is a free financial literacy series presented by public libraries throughout the Chicagoland area. To support mission of providing access to reliable information, these virtual events offer practical tips and advice from experienced financial advisors.

title: Financial Fitness: Budgeting and Savings Basics with Ryan Decker [Z]
description: Presented as part of the Financial Fitness 2026 series, this online program features financial professional Ryan Decker, discussing budgeting, saving, investing, and debt management. Participants will also receive resources for further learning.This virtual program is part of Financial Fitness 2026, a free financial literacy series presented by BIG (Business Interest Group),a group of business librarians in the Chicago suburbs in support of the libraries’ mission of providing access to reliable information; these virtual programs offer practical tips and advice from experienced financial advisors.Register Here!

title: Poetry Group of Evanston Writers Workshop
description:
Evanston Writers Workshop
The Evanston Writers Workshop Poetry Group will be meeting on the second Thursday of the month, from 7-9 P.M, at Sketchbook Brewery in Evanston.

People may submit 1-2 poems per person for critique via our submission form at the following url:
https://form.jotform.com/251125391424147

To find the poems we'll be reviewing for this month's meeting, visit our group website, https://evanstonwritersworkshop.org/ and look for the link to our submissions file drive under "Calendar and Submissions". The submitted poems will be accessible the week of the event.

title: Gardening for the Seasons
description: Set within the contemporary design of Millennium Park and against the urban backdrop of downtown Chicago, Lurie Garden has earned a reputation as a premier example of naturalistic garden design. Using the garden as our inspiration and drawing from the masterful vision of its perennial plant designer, Piet Oudolf, this talk presented by Kathryn Deery will explore a fundamental shift in our approach to gardening. We will move our focus from routine maintenance to thoughtful management, delve into the art of designing with both ornamental and native plants, and share advice for cutting back your garden to support wildlife and insects. With a few simple adjustments to the way we garden, a beautifully designed space that considers sustainability and ecology is within everyone’s reach.Kathryn serves as the director of horticulture for Lurie Garden and Millennium Park. Driven by a passion for the vital role public gardens play in communities, she works to maintain the design integrity of Lurie Garden while facilitating its continuous evolution. She also ensures that Millennium Park maintains a high standard of horticulture by prioritizing both its beauty and its ecological function.This event will be recorded and will be accessible on our YouTube events channel the following week.

title: Screenplay Critique Group
description:
Evanston Writers Workshop
Welcome to the Evanston Writers Workshop’s **Screenplay Critique Group**. We're a group of professional and aspiring writers who help each other polish their writing through workshop style critique. Writers have the opportunity to receive and give direct, constructive, and actionable feedback from attentive readers and writers who understand the craft of screenwriting.

## Details

**\- This group is for Screenwriters writers\, aged 18\+ at** **INTERMEDIATE to ADVANCED levels only**.

* We will meet monthly on **the second Tuesday of the month, from 7:00-9:00pm central time on ZOOM**.
\- We will critique up to 3 submissions \(up to 10 pages in Standard Screenplay Format\) per meeting\.
\- You do not need to be a member to attend or critique\, however **you need to be a member in order to submit and have your own work critiqued**.
\- This is a GIVE & TAKE group\. In exchange for giving thoughtful critique on others’ work\, you get thoughtful critique for your own\.
\- You must RSVP to each Screenplay Critique Group session in order to receive the submissions we will be critiquing\.
\- If you have a submission to be critiqued:

1. To submit, please [CLICK HERE](https://form.jotform.com/251125391424147) to upload your submissions or type
https://form.jotform.com/251125391424147 into your browser **by midnight the Friday before critique meeting**.
2. Put "**Screenplay Critique Group Submission” in the subject header.** (We have many different critique groups and want to make sure it’s directed to the correct folder.)
3. Include **your email address** and **short** bio (this group is for intermediate and advanced screenplay writers) inside your document so participants know where to send their notes.

EWW is a not-for-profit 501 (C) 3 organization founded in 2007. For more information about Evanston Writers Workshop, and membership, visit [evanstonwritersworkshop.org.](http://evanstonwritersworkshop.org./) We have critique groups for all writing levels and genres including for fiction, memoir/non-fiction, and poetry that meet in person in Evanston, IL and also online.
*Friendly reminder: please send your submissions by the Friday (midnight) before the meeting.*
