description: Join us for a waste-related presentation highlighting programming changes in Winnetka, including the introduction of the Recycle Coach app, the Hefty ReNew curbside collection for hard-to-recycle soft plastics, and the sunsetting of the polystyrene drop-off collection at Winnetka Public Works. Christina Seibert, Executive Director of the Solid Waste Agency of Northern Cook County, and Elyse Steiner, Sustainability Coordinator for the Village of Winnetka, will co-present with Liz Kunkle of Go Green Winnetka and Recycle Coach. This event is sponsored by Go Green Winnetka.

title: The History of Billy Joel
description: Though Billy Joel has not released a new rock album in over 27 years, he remains one of the world’s most popular live performers.  Using performance and interview video clips we’ll trace the arc of Billy’s career from his humble beginnings in Hicksville, NY to his slow rise to fame and his long string of hits including “Just The Way You Are”, ”It’s Still Rock N Roll To Me”, ”Uptown Girl” and “River Of Dreams” to his love life including his marriage to supermodel Christie Brinkley and finally to his current status as one of the world’s most successful touring acts.  “If you’re in the mood for a melody…the Piano Man will make you feel alright.”

Welcome to the Evanston Writers Workshop Critique Group. One of two foundational programs of EWW, the Critique Group is a group of professional and aspiring writers who help each other polish their writing. Through workshop-style critique, writers have the opportunity to receive direct feedback from attentive readers who understand the craft of writing. For more information, weekly submission links, as well as submission guidelines and requirements, please visit [https://evanstonwritersworkshop.org/submissionguidlines](https://evanstonwritersworkshop.org/submissionguidlines) or our website at [www.evannstonwritersworkshop.org](www.evannstonwritersworkshop.org) .

Friendly reminder: please have your submissions uploaded by the Friday (midnight) before the meeting.

title: Between the Lines: The Funeral Ladies of Ellerie County by Claire Swinarski
location: 6140 Lincoln Avenue, Morton Grove, IL, 60053, US
description: Between the Lines is now held on two different days, either in person or on Zoom. Titles are always available on Hoopla, as well as other library ebook platforms. New members are warmly welcome! Book Synopsis: Armed with a Crock-Pot and a pile of recipes, a grandmother, her granddaughter, and a mysterious young man work to bring a community together in this uplifting novel for readers of The Chicken Sisters.Esther Larson has been cooking for funerals in the Northwoods of Wisconsin for seventy years. Known locally as the “funeral ladies,” she and her cohort have worked hard to keep the mourners of Ellerie County fed—it is her firm belief that there is very little a warm casserole and a piece of cherry pie can’t fix. But, after falling for an internet scam that puts her home at risk, the proud Larson family matriarch is the one in need of help these days.Iris, Esther’s whip-smart Gen Z granddaughter, would do anything for her family and her community. As she watches her friends and family move out of their lakeside town onto bigger and better things, Iris wonders why she feels so left behind in the place she is desperate to make her home. But when Cooper Welsh shows up, she finally starts to feel like she’s found the missing piece of her puzzle.Cooper is dealing with becoming a legal guardian to his younger half-sister after his beloved stepmother dies. While their celebrity-chef father is focused on his booming career and top-ranked television show, Cooper is still hurting from a public tragedy he witnessed last year as a paramedic and finding it hard to cope. With Iris in the gorgeous Ellerie County, though, he hopes he might finally find the home he’s been looking for.It doesn’t seem like a community cookbook could possibly solve their problems, especially one where casseroles have their own section and cream of chicken soup mix is the most frequently used ingredient. But when you mix the can-do spirit of Midwestern grandmothers with the stubborn hope of a boy raised by food plus a dash of long-awaited forgiveness—things might just turn out okay.

title: Illinois Libraries Present - Cristina Henríquez:  Voice, Culture, and Human Connection
description: Join critically acclaimed, bestselling author, Cristina Henríquez, for a compelling conversation about her work. Henríquez’s newest book, The Great Divide, is a moving exploration of the people who lived, loved, and labored during the construction of the Panama Canal. Named a New York Times’ Editors’ Choice selection and TIME Magazine “100 Must-Read Books of 2024,” The Great Divide explores history and adversity in a place very special to her – her father’s homeland of Panama.Henríquez has also authored The Book of Unknown Americans, The World in Half, and Come Together, Fall Apart, all to significant acclaim. Her fiction has appeared in The New Yorker, The Atlantic, The New York Times, and The Best American Short Stories 2018, and she is a recipient of the 21st Century Award given by The Chicago Public Library Foundation. She is a graduate of Northwestern University and the Iowa Writer’s Workshop, and lives in the Chicago suburbs with her family.Carmen Álvarez will join Henríquez as moderator. Álvarez is an advocate for libraries and Latinx representation in the publishing industry. Her work has appeared in Glamour, Elle, and Vogue and she has a social media presence surpassing 180k followers across platforms.Illinois Libraries Present (ILP) is a Member based initiative of the Illinois Library Association and a statewide collaboration among public libraries offering premier events. Thank you to our state partners for the continuous support.Partial funding for this season is supported by a grant, awarded by the Illinois State Library, a Department of the Secretary of State, using funds provided by the U.S. Institute of Museum and Library Services, under the provisions of the Library Services and Technology Act (LSTA).ASL Interpreting Services are made possible by the support of Illinois Heartland Library System (IHLS) and Reaching Across Illinois Library System (RAILS).

Named a New York Times Editors’ Choice selection and TIME Magazine “100 Must-Read Books of 2024,” The Great Divide explores history and adversity in a place very special to her–her father’s homeland of Panama. This program will not be shown in person at the library. Register Here!This is an online event, made possible by Illinois Libraries Present, a statewide collaboration between public libraries, including Lincolnwood Public Library.

title: Annual Meeting and Program: Songs for Spring
description:
Alliance Française du North Shore
**Annual Meeting for Alliance Française Members**
Required by Alliance Française du North Shore bylaws, this short members' meeting will convene at approximately 2:15 PM, immediately following the monthly program. This meeting will include approval of the proposed 2026-2027 budget and the election of members of the board of directors. Members of the AFduNS as of April 1 are eligible to vote in the election and on other matters.

If you're unable to attend the annual meeting, please complete this proxy ballot (link to come) **by 10 AM on Monday, April 20**.

**Program**
Singer and guitarist Kevin Soucie returns to share his extensive repertoire, including songs by Jacques Brel, Édith Piaf, Charles Trenet, Francis Cabrel, Charles Aznavour and many others. This intimate performance will transport you to the cafés of Paris with timeless melodies. [Click here for the program selections.](https://storage.googleapis.com/production-ipage-v1-0-0/830/1512830/9C8FaW1X/5b3eb831f2be4f27a020c51e7e705663?fileName=Music%20Program%202026.pdf) You won’t be able to help but sing along.

Kevin Soucie travels the country performing timeless, popular and contemporary French songs for francophone audiences. A native of Milwaukee, Soucie was a student in Québec and in Europe, and also served as a Peace Corps volunteer in Senegal. Hearing French and Québecois songs along the way, Soucie was inspired to sing and play *chansons* with his guitar—a hobby that became a passion.

**Program Registration for Members of the AF DuPage: Free**
If you're a member of the AF DuPage and are attending a monthly program for the first time, please RSVP to programmes@AFnorthshore.org with “DuPage” in the subject line. Include your name and Zoom screen name, if different. If you're a member of our Meetup group, we'll mark you as such and you'll no longer need to send RSVP emails as long as you RSVP on Meetup.
If you're not a member of the AFduNS Meetup group, you'll need to send an email RSVP for each monthly program.

**Program Registration for Nonmembers: $10**
If you're not a member of the AFduNS or the AF DuPage, the cost of attending this program is $10. [Click here](https://forms.gle/f89WGYhPkj867tYi8) to register and pay online with PayPal by 8 PM the day before the program. There will be no refunds or credits.

We appreciate reservations by 8 PM the day before the program. Walk-ins may pay at the door on their mobile device.

**Membership in the AFduNS**
While no membership is required for newcomers, we welcome new members whose membership will support the activities of the AFduNS, a nonprofit 501(c)(3) organization. For further info, visit our [website](https://afnorthshore.org/events) or [click here to contact us](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScRxM03DpryO_lZlGWK-aDOCiFHFbivJimTScMOQmWk7KgOJQ/viewform?pli=1). title: Movie Night
description: Small Things Like TheseBased on the book by Claire Keegan. Set in 1985 Christmastime in the Irish Village New Ross, the story follows coal merchant Bill Furlong, a hardworking father of five daughters. While delivering coal to a local convent, he witnesses harsh treatment of a young girl and later, while making an earlier than usual delivery to the convent, he discovers teenaged Sarah locked in the shed. These events lead to a flashback to his own difficult childhood, growing up the son of an unwed mother. What can he do in the present, especially in a small community that is afraid to challenge the status quo? Starring Cillian Murphy, Eileen Walsh, and Emily Watson.After watching the film, share your questions and comments in a discussion with Chris and Sharon.2024 : 98 minutes : Rated PG-13 for thematic material : Drama.Free tickets will be given to those who attend.Watch the trailer for Small Things Like These.Find Small Things Like These in the library catalog.

title: Between the Lines: The Funeral Ladies of Ellerie County by Claire Swinarski (Online)
description: Between the Lines meets for online discussions via Zoom. Registered patrons will receive an email the day before the program with the meeting ID and password for joining the event. New members are warmly welcome!Titles are always available on Hoopla, as well as other library ebook platforms. Contact us for assistance, or find any instructions you may need on our Download & Stream page.Book Synopsis: Armed with a Crock-Pot and a pile of recipes, a grandmother, her granddaughter, and a mysterious young man work to bring a community together in this uplifting novel for readers of The Chicken Sisters.Esther Larson has been cooking for funerals in the Northwoods of Wisconsin for seventy years. Known locally as the “funeral ladies,” she and her cohort have worked hard to keep the mourners of Ellerie County fed—it is her firm belief that there is very little a warm casserole and a piece of cherry pie can’t fix. But, after falling for an internet scam that puts her home at risk, the proud Larson family matriarch is the one in need of help these days.Iris, Esther’s whip-smart Gen Z granddaughter, would do anything for her family and her community. As she watches her friends and family move out of their lakeside town onto bigger and better things, Iris wonders why she feels so left behind in the place she is desperate to make her home. But when Cooper Welsh shows up, she finally starts to feel like she’s found the missing piece of her puzzle.Cooper is dealing with becoming a legal guardian to his younger half-sister after his beloved stepmother dies. While their celebrity-chef father is focused on his booming career and top-ranked television show, Cooper is still hurting from a public tragedy he witnessed last year as a paramedic and finding it hard to cope. With Iris in the gorgeous Ellerie County, though, he hopes he might finally find the home he’s been looking for.It doesn’t seem like a community cookbook could possibly solve their problems, especially one where casseroles have their own section and cream of chicken soup mix is the most frequently used ingredient. But when you mix the can-do spirit of Midwestern grandmothers with the stubborn hope of a boy raised by food plus a dash of long-awaited forgiveness—things might just turn out okay.

title: Retirement Reality with Jill Schlesinger
description: Are you thinking about retirement? Does it seem completely out of reach? CBS News Business Analyst Jill Schlesinger, will walk through the specific steps necessary to get you where you want to go, whether you are just starting off in your career or ready to wind down. Jill takes the mystery out of finances to help you take control of your own.Jill Schlesinger, CFP, a winner of the Emmy Award and Gracie Award, translates complicated business and economic news into understandable, relatable topics for everyday viewers and listeners. She covers the economy, markets, investing and anything else with a dollar sign on TV, podcasts, and radio. Jill also won a 2018 Personal Finance Reporting Award from the Radio Television Digital News Association/National Endowment for Financial Education.This webinar is part of Financial Fitness 2026. Financial Fitness is a free financial literacy series presented by BIG (Business Interest Group), a group of business librarians in the Chicago suburbs. In support of the libraries’ mission of providing access to reliable information, these virtual programs offer practical tips and advice from experienced financial advisors.Register here to receive a Zoom link. After you register, you will receive a confirmation email in the form of a calendar invite. If you have any questions after you register, please contact ILfinancialfitness@gmail.com.

title: MGPL After Dark: The Librarians
location: 6140 Lincoln Avenue, Morton Grove, IL, 60053, US
description: In this award winning documentary, librarians across the US examine how book review policies and collection standards that restrict race-related and LGBTQIA+ content are shaping education and communities.Join us for a special screening of this documentary as part of National Library Week. Light refreshments will be served at 6:00 pm. More about the film: When Texas lawmakers seek to review a list of books, librarians find themselves on the frontlines of a national battle. Across the U.S., librarians face the impact of uniting against library collection standards that include restrictions on race-related and LGBTQIA+ content. Drawing on historical context, The Librarians explores the broader implications for education and public life. (2025, NR, 1hr 35min) About Indie Lens Pop-UpIndie Lens Pop-Up is a community series that brings people together for film screenings and conversations. Featuring documentaries seen on PBS's Independent Lens, Indie Lens Pop-Up draws local residents, leaders, and organizations to discuss what matters most. For more information, visit pbs.org/indielenspopup.

title: #114: Nervous Conditions by Tsitsi Dangarembga
description:
Black Girls Read Book Club
**Our April book selection is *Nervous Conditions* by Tsitsi Dangarembga!**

About the book: The groundbreaking first novel in Tsitsi Dangarembga’s award-winning trilogy, *Nervous Conditions*, won the Commonwealth Writers Prize and has been “hailed as one of the 20th century’s most significant works of African literature” (*The New York Times*). Two decades before Zimbabwe would win independence and ended white minority rule, thirteen-year-old Tambudzai Sigauke embarks on her education. On her shoulders rest the economic hopes of her parents, siblings, and extended family, and within her burns the desire for independence. She yearns to be free of the constraints of her rural village and thinks she’s found her way out when her wealthy uncle offers to sponsor her schooling. But she soon learns that the education she receives at his mission school comes with a price.

**Support Black Girls Read Chicago by purchasing *[Nervous Conditions](https://bookshop.org/a/25397/9781644450710)* on Bookshop (affiliate link).**

description: The Students for Global Health and the Sustainability Club will host health screenings at both campuses. At these screenings, students will receive their values of blood pressure, blood oxygen levels, blood glucose levels, blood hemoglobin levels, and blood cholesterol and triglyceride levels. We will also provide nutritional and exercise information to help keep these values within normal values.

title: Monday Matinees
location: 4000 W. Pratt Ave., Lincolnwood, IL, 60712, US
description: Enjoy great films at your library every Monday! Doors open at 12:45pm.  Eternity (2025)Mon, Apr 6 • 1-3pm • PG-13, 112 mins • [D]In an afterlife where souls have one week to decide where to spend eternity, Joan is faced with the impossible choice between the man she spent her life with and her first love, who died young and has waited decades for her to arrive. Sinners (2025)Mon, Apr 13 • 1-3:30pm • R, 128 mins • [D]Trying to leave their troubled lives behind, twin brothers return to their hometown to start again, only to discover that an even greater evil is waiting to welcome them back. Song Sung Blue (2025)Mon, Apr 20 • 1-3:30pm • PG-13, 133 mins • [D]Two down-on-their-luck performers form a Neil Diamond tribute band, proving it's never too late to find love and follow your dreams. Hamnet (2025)Mon, Apr 27 • 1-3:30pm • PG-13, 125 mins • [D]William Shakespeare and his wife, Agnes, celebrate the birth of their son, Hamnet. However, when tragedy strikes and Hamnet dies at a young age, it inspires Shakespeare to write his timeless masterpiece Hamlet.

title: Civic Love
description: Outside in our south courtyard with cozy fire vibes, see how civic love appears in your life and how you can strengthen relationships in your community.36 Questions for Civic Love is a tool created by the National Public Housing Museum (NPHM), based on psychologist Arthur Aron and Detroit-based organizer Grace Lee Boggs’ work. According to the NPHM, “Civic love is one’s love for society, expressed through a commitment to the common good. It is a belief in the idea that we’re all better off, when we are all better off." In this event, you will connect with friends and neighbors and learn how you can experience and grow the practice of civic love. Keyword: USA 250

title: Books in the Woods (R) (Hybrid)
location: 4000 W. Pratt Ave., Lincolnwood, IL, 60712, US
description: This month we will be reading What Strange Paradise by Omar El Akkad.This novel is the story of Amir, a Syrian refugee who washes up on an island after the ship he was escaping on collapses, and Vanna, the teenage girl native to the island who finds him. Though Vanna and Amir are complete strangers, though they don't speak a common language, Vanna is determined to do whatever it takes to save the boy.Registration required to receive Zoom information.

title: Readpool: Salamandre by I.N.J. Culbard (Online)
description: Readpool is an adult graphic novel discussion group with a focus on challenging and acclaimed stories.Book Synopsis: Kaspar Salamandre is a bereaved young artist who is sent to stay with his enigmatic grandfather in a land ruled under an oppressive regime, where there can be only one loved one: The Emperor. In this land where flowers are contraband, music is illegal, and art is created in hiding, Kaspar discovers a world of art revolutionaries, espionage and the Secret Police. His search for answers will bring him face to face with the meaning of sacrifice. But, will anything bring him closer to overcoming his loss?If you need assistance with getting a copy of the book, please contact Nate Miller at nmiller@mgpl.org or at 847-929-5115.

title: ELL Literacy Circle
description: Have you ever wanted to be part of a library book discussion group, but you worried that your English language skills were not strong enough?In this class for advanced-level English learners, we will read and discuss an American novel together, with the support of Margaret Feit Clarke, an experienced ESL teacher from the ELL Parent Center. You will strengthen your English skills while learning more about great literature, American culture, and the Skokie community. Limited to 16 participants. Free tickets will be given to those who attend.The class will meet on Tuesdays for 12 weeks: March 3-May 12, 9:30-11:30 am. No class March 31.We’ll read and talk about Olive, Again by Elizabeth Strout. The book offers a series of interconnected stories that provide a portrait of a complicated woman confronting her own mortality.Find Olive, Again in the library catalog.Find additional titles by Elizabeth Strout in the library catalog.

description: Gary Livacari presents a look at the life and times of Babe Ruth, from his difficult early childhood and years at St. Mary’s Industrial School for Boys, to his rise as one of baseball’s most iconic figures. title: Historical Examinations of Women in Western Civilizations in the Second World War and Beyond: A Virtual Conference
description:
Over two evenings, this conference explores women's experiences in Western Civilizations during and after the Second World War. The examination will include several countries and situations.

The conference starts on April 29, 2026, at 6 p.m. with introductory remarks by Wendy Adele-Marie, Ph.D., Distinguished Professor of History and Coordinator of Jewish Studies at Oakton College.  After each evening session, there will be time for Q&A. Rija Quereshi will deliver the closing statement at the end of the conference on April 30.

The conference proceedings will be published in Adele-Marie’s forthcoming second edition of her OER workbook, History of Women in Western Civilizations. Oakton will also offer the course Women in Western Civilizations (HIS 236) online and asynchronously in the summer of 2026 to further complement the conference.

Please register in advance to attend the two-day virtual conference; the same link serves for both nights. All of the times for the event are in Chicago time. You can check time zones to help you register. Please note that the times of each session are subject to change. For more information about this event, please contact the conference organizer, Wendy Adele-Marie, at wadelemarie@oakton.edu.

This event is sponsored by Jewish Studies and the EGHPS Department at Oakton College. This event is also made possible by a generous grant from the Oakton Educational Foundation.

title: Mystery Book Club
description: This month we'll be discussing Death at La Fenice by Donna Leon. "Ladies and gentleman, I regret to inform you that Maestro Wellauer is unable to continue the performance." Someone gave the world’s foremost operatic conductor a dose of cyanide during Intermission. Vice-commissario Guido Brunetti is on the case–but the solution may force him to choose between his duty to the law and his own sense of justice. This is the first book in Donna Leon’s beloved series about a detective who is “Venetian to the bone.”Find Death at La Fenice in the library catalog.

title: America's Oddest Museums - Film Screening
description: Join multi-Emmy Award winning producer and editor Harvey Moshman for a screening of a 1-hour special of the PBS series Wild Travels. When it comes to museums, there’s the kind your school made you go to on field trips, but what about the quirkier variety that feature collections of mustard, or pinball machines, or even just dust; that showcase action figures, or portraits made entirely from bugs? The kind of museums that are located in motel lobbies, or in homemade castles, or abandoned barns? Learn about them all in AMERICA'S ODDEST MUSEUMS. Screening followed by live Q&A.

title: Young Steinway Concert
description: Alison Chung, a senior at Glenbrook North High School, studies violin with Desirée Ruhstrat. Alison was recently named runner-up in the ADM Concerto Competition and performed the first movement of the Walton Violin Concerto with the Kishwaukee Symphony Orchestra. As a member of Quartetto Speranza, she received the Bronze Medal in the Junior Division at the 2025 Fischoff National Chamber Music Competition. With the Phoenix Quartet, she earned second prize at the 2026 Rembrandt Chamber Music Competition and first prize in the Open Division of the Chicago National Chamber Music Competition.Duo Dunamis consists of pianists David Goetz and Ryan Hu. David is a junior at Loyola Academy and Ryan is a junior at North Shore Country Day School. Both have been awarded first place for both duo and solo performance in the American Protege Music Competition and performed at Weill Recital Hall at Carnegie Hall. The duo has appeared in the Chicago Duo Piano Music Festival, the Plymouth Concert Series, and the Merion Concert Series.Pianist Pratyay Rao is a junior at New Trier Township High School. He studies piano with Lyudmila Lakisova. Pratyay won second place in the 2025 American Fine Arts Festival International Concerto Competition, was a finalist in 2024 New Trier Concerto Competition, and won first place in the 2024 Charleston Summer Music Competition. Pratyay also participates in Science Olympiad and DECA competitions.Free tickets will be given to those present starting at 2:30 pm.

title: Morning Movie
description: Elio Eleven-year-old Elio believes that extraterrestrials are out there and feels he’d fit in better with them. One day, his dream comes true: he's taken to the Communiverse and is mistaken for an ambassador from Earth. This fun, action-packed cosmic misadventure explores themes of loneliness, belonging, grief, loss, family, acceptance, self-discovery, and identity. Elio and his space-alien best friend Glordon are endearing and relatable, and the animation is bright and imaginative. Featuring the voice talents of Yonas Kibreab, Remy Edgerly, Zoe Saldana, Brad Garrett, Jameela Jamil, Shirley Henderson, and Matthias Schweighofer. Directed by Madeline Sharafian, Domee Shi, and Adrian Molina. 2026 Appointment Dates:

February 4March 4April 1May 6

Appointments are available from 12:00 - 2:00 p.m.

Please email OAEDsupport@oakton.edu or call 847.376.7298 if you have any questions or would like to schedule an appointment. You must make an appointment. Walk-ins are not accepted.

title: Spring Migration Birding Walk
description: Join Red Hill Birding's founder Josh Engel at the Skokie Lagoons to learn about bird migration and see spring migrants at one of our best local birding hotspots. The beginning of April is a transitional period when a wide variety of species are passing through on their way north, resulting in high bird diversity and a large number of birds. Our walk will be easy, slow-paced, and educational, and hopefully we will see lots of birds! Bring binoculars if you have them, but if you don't, that's no problem.Please meet us at the north end of the parking lot off of Willow Road here:https://maps.app.goo.gl/W1EAkqmLoonUWQyY6

title: Thriving Through Menopause
description: Menopause is a natural transition, but that doesn’t mean you have to suffer through it. Kristin Dold Bennett, DO, FACOG, leads a practical, evidence-based conversation about perimenopause and menopause. She'll address hot flashes, brain fog, sleep disruption, libido changes, and treatment options tailored to individual needs. Walk away informed, empowered, and confident.Dr. Bennett is a board-certified OB-GYN at Midwest Center for Women’s HealthCare in Skokie and Buffalo Grove. She provides comprehensive women’s health care, including preventive gynecology, annual exams, contraception counseling, prenatal care, and menopause management. She is dedicated to personalized, patient-centered obstetric and gynecologic care for women at every stage of life. Learn more at https://mcwhc.com/providers/dr-kristie-dold-bennett-obgyn/Keyword: Health & Wellness

title: Movie Night
description: Agent of HappinessBhutan measures happiness via the Gross National Happiness (GNH) index. Amber, a happiness agent working for the government, surveys people across the country, asking questions to ascertain their happiness levels. Documentarians Arun Bhattarai and Dorottya Zurbó follow Amber not only through his work and the diverse people he interviews, but also through his personal life. The result is a touching and thought-provoking exploration of happiness and sadness, dreams and reality, prosperity and hardship, companionship and loneliness, as well as a subtle scrutiny of the concept of GNH. Directed by Arun Bhattarai and Dorottya Zurbó.After watching the film, share your questions and comments in a discussion with Chris and Sharon.2024 : 94 minutes : Not rated: DocumentaryIn Nepali and Dzongkha with English subtitles.Free tickets will be given to those who attend.Watch the trailer for Agent of Happiness.Find Agent of Happiness in the library catalog.

title: Own the Interview: Preparation That Reduces Anxiety
description: Interviews feel uncertain, and uncertainty creates anxiety. This workshop shows how focused preparation reduces both. You’ll build a story vault of accomplishments to make your value clear; create confident first lines with powerful, intentional language; and sketch a simple 90-day plan that signals your readiness. You'll learn to apply powerful, intentional language so that you start your answers with confidence and how to implement a simple system with a workbook to stay consistent and in control.Presented on Zoom in partnership with the Lake Cook Career Collaborative. The presentation will be recorded and available for viewing afterward. Register and receive a Zoom link.

title: Babel-On with R.F. Kuang
description: R.F. Kuang’s genre-bending fiction broaches ordinarily serious topics from a satirical and fantastical perspective. Academic yet approachable, her work combines history, magic, and classical literary tradition to render powerful critiques of academia, the publishing industry, and even contemporary popular culture. Her most recent novel, Katabasis, follows two graduate students as they descend into hell after the death of their professor. The screen rights options to the novel were sold to Amazon MGM Studios for an upcoming TV series before its publication. Her other bestselling titles include Yellowface, Babel, and The Poppy War trilogy, and she is the recipient of the Nebula Award for Best Novel, the Goodreads Choice Awards for Best Fiction, and the American Book Award. Kelly Jensen, an anti-censorship advocate, a senior editor at Book Riot, and writer who has compiled such anthologies as Here We Are: Feminism for the Real World and (Don’t) Call Me Crazy, will be leading the conversation.This event is made possible by Illinois Libraries Present (ILP), a statewide collaboration among public libraries offering premier events. ILP is funded in part by a grant awarded by the Illinois State Library, a department of the Office of Secretary of State, using funds provided by the U.S. Institute of Museum and Library Services, under the provisions of the Library Services and Technology Act. ILP is committed to inclusion and accessibility. To request accommodations, please email illinoislibrariespresent@ila.org. Register and receive a Zoom link for this event.

title: Babel-On with R.F. Kuang (Online)
description: Award-winning, New York Times bestselling author R.F. Kuang, creator of The Poppy War trilogy, Babel, and Yellowface, will discuss her genre-bending fiction that blends history, magic, and sharp cultural critique.Register hereThis event is made possible by Illinois Libraries Present, a statewide collaboration between public libraries offering high-quality events.

Kuang’s other bestselling titles include Yellowface, Babel, and The Poppy War trilogy, and she is the recipient of the Nebula Award for Best Novel, the Goodreads Choice Awards for Best Fiction, and the American Book Award.Kelly Jensen, anti-censorship advocate, Senior Editor at Book Riot, and writer who has compiled such anthologies as Here We Are: Feminism for the Real World and (Don’t) Call Me Crazy, will be joining Kuang in conversation.Illinois Libraries Present (ILP) is a Member based initiative of the Illinois Library Association and a statewide collaboration among public libraries offering premier events. title: Between the Lines: The Mighty Red by Louise Erdrich
location: 6140 Lincoln Avenue, Morton Grove, IL, 60053, US
description: Between the Lines is now held on two different days, either in person or on Zoom. Titles are always available on Hoopla, as well as other library ebook platforms. New members are warmly welcome! Book Synopsis: In this stunning novel, Pulitzer Prize and National Book Award–winning author Louise Erdrich tells a story of love, natural forces, spiritual yearnings, and the tragic impact of uncontrollable circumstances on ordinary people’s lives.In the Red River Valley of North Dakota, several lives revolve around a wedding fraught with desire, jealousy, and uncertainty. Gary Geist, a terrified young man set to inherit two farms, is desperate to marry Kismet Poe, an impulsive, lapsed goth who can’t read her own future but will settle for fulfilling his. Her best friend, Hugo, a gentle, red-haired, homeschooled giant, also loves Kismet and is determined to steal her away and build a life together. Kismet’s mother, Crystal, drives a truck for Gary’s family, and on her nightly runs, tunes in to the darkness of late-night radio, experiences visions of guardian angels, and worries about what’s to come, for her daughter and herself.The Mighty Red is Louise Erdrich at her consummate best. A novel of tender humor, disquietude, yearning, community, and family, it is about ordinary people who dream, grow up, fall in love, struggle, endure tragedy, carry bitter secrets; men and women both complicated and contradictory, flawed and decent, lonely and hopeful. Human time, deep time, Red River time, and geological time are explored alongside the impact of crises in our own time—climate change, the depletion of natural resources, the economic meltdown of 2008. It is a story about our tattered bond with the earth, and about love in all of its absurdity and splendor.

title: Friday Film
description: HamnetThis lush, emotional historical drama—winner of the Golden Globe for Best Drama—is based on the critically acclaimed novel by Maggie O'Farrell, who cowrote the screenplay with Oscar-winning director Chloé Zhao. Set in 16th-century Tudor England, the film follows Agnes, a gifted healer, and her husband Will, whose marriage is pushed to the breaking point by the sudden death of their 11-year-old son, Hamnet, from the plague. Agnes grapples with a raw grief that threatens to consume her, while Will ultimately pours his profound sorrow into the creation of his immortal masterpiece, Hamlet.Starring Jessie Buckley, Paul Mescal, Emily Watson, and Joe Alwyn. Directed by Chloé Zhao. Hamnet received eight Academy Award nominations, including Best Picture, Director, Actress (Buckley), and Adapted Screenplay. 2025 : 126 minutes : Rated PG-13 for thematic content, some strong sexuality, and partial nudity : Historical dramaFree tickets will be given to those who attend.Watch a trailer for Hamnet.Find Hamnet in the library catalog.

title: Between the Lines: The Mighty Red by Louise Erdrich (Online)
description: Between the Lines meets for online discussions via Zoom. Registered patrons will receive an email the day before the program with the meeting ID and password for joining the event. New members are warmly welcome!Titles are always available on Hoopla, as well as other library ebook platforms. Contact us for assistance, or find any instructions you may need on our Download & Stream page.Book Synopsis: In this stunning novel, Pulitzer Prize and National Book Award–winning author Louise Erdrich tells a story of love, natural forces, spiritual yearnings, and the tragic impact of uncontrollable circumstances on ordinary people’s lives.In the Red River Valley of North Dakota, several lives revolve around a wedding fraught with desire, jealousy, and uncertainty. Gary Geist, a terrified young man set to inherit two farms, is desperate to marry Kismet Poe, an impulsive, lapsed goth who can’t read her own future but will settle for fulfilling his. Her best friend, Hugo, a gentle, red-haired, homeschooled giant, also loves Kismet and is determined to steal her away and build a life together. Kismet’s mother, Crystal, drives a truck for Gary’s family, and on her nightly runs, tunes in to the darkness of late-night radio, experiences visions of guardian angels, and worries about what’s to come, for her daughter and herself.The Mighty Red is Louise Erdrich at her consummate best. A novel of tender humor, disquietude, yearning, community, and family, it is about ordinary people who dream, grow up, fall in love, struggle, endure tragedy, carry bitter secrets; men and women both complicated and contradictory, flawed and decent, lonely and hopeful. Human time, deep time, Red River time, and geological time are explored alongside the impact of crises in our own time—climate change, the depletion of natural resources, the economic meltdown of 2008. It is a story about our tattered bond with the earth, and about love in all of its absurdity and splendor.

title: Fantasy Book Club

When her magic makes itself known, her existence is discovered and she is forced to run away. While fleeing, she meets the Crown Prince, who invites her to train alongside him in archery and magic.This fantasy book is inspired by the legend of the Chinese moon goddess, Chang'e, and is the first in the Celestial Kingdom duology.Find Daughter of the Moon Goddess in the library catalog.

title: Morning Movie
description: Elio Eleven-year-old Elio believes that extraterrestrials are out there and feels he’d fit in better with them than with people. One day, his dream comes true. He's taken to the Communiverse and mistaken for an ambassador from Earth. This fun action-packed cosmic misadventure explores themes of loneliness, belonging, grief, loss, family, acceptance, self-discovery, and identity. Elio and his space-alien best friend Glordon are endearing and relatable, and the animation is bright and imaginative. Featuring the voice talents of Yonas Kibreab, Remy Edgerly, Zoe Saldana, Brad Garrett, Jameela Jamil, Shirley Henderson, and Matthias Schweighofer. Directed by Madeline Sharafian, Domee Shi, and Adrian Molina. This film received an Oscar nomination for Best Animated Feature Film. 2025 : 98 minutes : Rated PG for some action/peril and thematic elements : AnimationFree tickets will be given to all who attend. Watch the trailer for Elio.Find Elio in the library catalog.

title: Pages Through History
description: This month we'll be discussing The Second Founding: How the Civil War and Reconstruction Remade the Constitution by Eric Foner. It would be hard for a person contemplating the United States of 1865 not to conclude that the system the Founders had bequeathed the nation just under a century earlier had been catastrophically flawed. Pulitzer Prize-winning historian Eric Foner lays out the ways the 13th, 14th, and 15th Amendments–all passed in just five years–represented an attempt to fix those flaws. They abolished slavery, codified the principles of birthright citizenship and legal equality, and guaranteed the right of Black men to vote. We live in the America the “Reconstruction Amendments” made, but we’ve been fighting about those amendments ever since.   Find The Second Founding in the library catalog.

description: We Bought a ZooBenjamin Mee, recently widowed with two children—14-year-old Dylan and 7-year-old Rosie—thinks that starting over will do them all good, especially after Dylan gets expelled from school. While looking to relocate, they find a dilapidated old house that happens to come with a zoo that needs saving. Despite the huge financial commitment and having no experience, Benjamin takes on the task with the help of his family.Loosely inspired by a true story, this is a healing adventure about second chances. title: Friday Film
description: Wicked: For GoodOnce more, we are in the wonderful world of Oz. Wicked: For Good is the second chapter of a two-part adaptation of the hit musical play. Indeed, times are turbulent, and all is not as it seems—the Wizard lies, propaganda spreads, and many innocent Ozians are being suppressed. Elphaba, rebellious as ever, fights against injustices and is branded as the Wicked Witch of the West; meanwhile, Glinda, driven by a need to conform and be liked, becomes the beloved Good Witch. Will the two—once-best friends, now public adversaries—find a way to work together and change Oz for the better?Starring Ariana Grande, Cynthia Erivo, Jonathan Bailey, Ethan Slater, Bowen Yang, Marissa Bode, Colman Domingo, Michelle Yeoh, and Jeff Goldblum. Directed by Jon M. Chu.2025 : 137 minutes : Rated PG-13 for action/violence, some suggestive material, and thematic material : MusicalFree tickets will be given to those who attend. Watch a trailer for Wicked: For Good.Find Wicked: For Good in the library catalog.

title: Songs of the Illinois Freedom Road
description: Award-winning folk musician and folklorist Chris Vallillo brings the music and the stories of the Underground Railroad in Illinois to life in this engaging and powerful show featuring first-person accounts of Freedom Seekers as well as performances and insights into the music that drove this historic movement. “While traditional accounts of the underground railroad generally dismiss Illinois, our state had more miles bordering slave states than any other” said Chris, “and those borders were rivers which made for excellent escape routes.”Chris Vallillo is a singer/songwriter and roots musician who makes the people and places of “unmetropolitan” America come to life in song. Having spent the last 30 years in the rural Midwest, he has a natural affinity for American roots music. He weaves original, contemporary, and traditional songs and narratives into a compelling and entertaining portrait of the history and lifestyles of the Midwest.Free tickets will be distributed to those present at 2:30 pm.

title: A Brief History of Skokie
description: Illinois became a state in 1818, but even by the beginning of the 1830s, Chicago, as the largest non-Indian settlement, only had about 150 inhabitants. Learn about the beginnings of Niles Township, the first settlers in the area and their relationship to the Native Americans, and how this place would eventually become Skokie -- with a new Village seal and a new Village flag!

title: Morning Movie
description: We Bought a ZooBenjamin Mee, recently widowed with two children—14-year-old Dylan and 7-year-old Rosie—thinks that starting over will do them all good, especially after Dylan gets expelled from school. While looking to relocate, they find a dilapidated old house that happens to come with a zoo that needs saving. There, they meet zookeeper Kelly Foster and her small, dedicated staff, who want to see it reopened. Despite the huge financial commitment and having no experience, Benjamin takes on the task with the help of his family.Loosely inspired by a true story, this is a healing adventure about second chances. Starring Matt Damon, Scarlett Johansson, Thomas Haden Church, Patrick Fugit, and Elle Fanning. Directed by Cameron Crowe.2011 : 124 minutes : Rated PG for language and some thematic elements : Comedy-dramaFree tickets will be given to all who attend. Watch the trailer for We Bought a Zoo.Find We Bought a Zoo in the library catalog.

title: Rohingya Music in Exile--Courage, Faith, and Love
description: The Crossing Borders Music String Quartet shares brand-new arrangements of Rohingya music and its stories of courage, faith, and love in exile. Developed after a month of field work in the Rohingya refugee camps of Cox's Bazar, Bangladesh-- the world's largest--this performance shares the melodies, hope, and humanity of Rohingya musicians who are the keepers of their people’s stories and cultural legacy. Transcend physical distance with music that helps everyone "see the Rohingya with our hearts" and celebrates our shared humanity. Free tickets will be distributed to those present at 2:30 pm.
